title: Excellent Hold, I'm Really Impressed
text:
I bought this specifically because of the anti-humidity and anti-frizz claims. I had an outdoor garden party to attend and we thought it might be damp. It turned out to be dry, so I can't speak on the humidity factor as of yet, but I can say I was extremely impressed regardless. I did my hair in the morning, traveled a couple hours away to a party where I sat outside all day long on a fairly windy day. Not strong winds, but more than a breeze for sure. I brought the bottle in my bag but I never needed to use it to touch up. I then drove home 16 hours later and went straight to bed because I was exhausted. I woke up the next day and my hair still looked good!

It's a fast drying "freeze-hold" type spray. It's a bit stiff to the touch but it doesn't look stiff at all, and it comes out easily later when you brush it. I have thinning, fine hair so it was important to me that my "strategic styling" to cover the thinner areas held in place and this did the trick, so I'm sold. The scent isn't pleasing but it's not bad either, a little perfumey but I can deal with it for the great hold I get. This is the best bottle of hairspray I've tried in the last couple of decades actually.

I bought mine at a local department store. It has been a godsend. No more hair in the bathroom sink. No more hair going down the drain -- and clogging pipes. Norelco got it right with this trimmer. Thankfully, they included a rechargeable battery since the vacuum does use a lot of power. No complaints about the length selector or noise. My cordless tooth brush is almost as loud.

title: Great moisturizer!
text:
I had been using the “Fractionated Coconut Oil by Sky Organics “for a few weeks now. The bottle arrived sealed in a plastic bag with a pumper top included. The bottle holds 16.0 oz. of coconut oil.
I purchased the coconut oil because I wanted to use it as a moisturizer on my feet. In the summer when I go barefoot most days outside, my feet can get really dry. I like to rub some coconut oil on them and wear socks to bed and in the morning my feet and smooth and soft once again. The coconut oil smells so good. I have been using it on my face and elbows as well. It is the perfect moisturizer.
I started using the coconut oil to remove eye makeup. I had been using Vaseline but I found that it takes more wiping and scrubbing to remove the makeup. The coconut oil takes it right off and moisturizes my eyes while doing so.
My friend was over the other day with her 6-month old boy and when she changed his diaper she realized he was getting a rash. She saw that I had the coconut oil and she asked if she could use it as a diaper cream. Who knew that coconut oil was also a diaper cream substitute. I love the coconut oil. I am going to use it in some of my cooking. The smell is incredible. I am going to use it in my hair as a leave in conditioner.

title: Not a 5 Star Curler
text:
This eyelash curler is well made, and it's a good one. With that being said I will have to say that after I read all the reviews on it before I bought it, I was expecting something fabulous, and really, this is just a really good eyelash curler, nothing more. I have owned eyelash curlers for many years and my last one was a Revlon brand that I paid less than $5.00 for and for the money, it was almost as good as the Bella one. I have no regrets on buying the Bella curler, and if you're considering it, I would say go for it, it does what it's supposed to, but it won't be a glorious experience by any means as it may appear from some of the other reviews. It will pinch your eyelid if you don't grasp your lashes correctly. It will crimp your lashes if you don't grasp/squeeze properly, as any eyelash curler will do, and as some of the other reviewers stated, the curl isn't long-lasting. As I said this is a good item, just don't expect miracles.

I'm hoping the pads will be more durable than the other brands I have owned and only time will tell on that.

title: Cute for birthday parties, weddings, or playing dress-up
text:
We go to a Renaissance Faire in the Spring & we've never dressed up but we see so many people there who wear costumes. One of the things that the girls & women wear are floral headpieces similar to these Flower Girl Headpieces for Weddings.
This is a set of 6 floral headpieces. Each one has 5 different colored Daisy-like flowers & a bunch of leaves attached to a green wire frame. They tie at the back of the head with a shimmery white ribbon so they can be adjusted to fit any head size. Each headpiece came individually wrapped in a plastic package & then the 6 packages were kind of stuffed into one big plastic package so they were a bit squashed when I received them. A couple of leaves fell off of 2 of the headpieces but it's a quick fix with a glue gun. In fact, if you want to make a fuller wreath, you could add more flowers with a glue gun as well.
These headpieces are perfect for a princess or fairy birthday party for your little girl, for the flower girl at a wedding, for wearing to a Renaissance Faire, playing dress-up or as a part of a costume.

title: Disappointed - went back to my T800
text:
I bought a T800 quite a few years ago when I found the Austrian-made unit on sale at the local Target store. It lasted very well, and I was somewhat disappointed when the battery eventually would no longer accept a charge, though it continued to work while plugged in.
After carefully looking into alternatives, I went with a T970 from Amazon.com, hoping for the same quality. Major letdown! This Chinese-made unit just is not up to the same standard as my T800. The charger is a seperate seperate and bulky transformer, instead of the simple cord of the T800. The T970 is larger, more awkward to hold and to use, and more fragile in every possible way. The vacuum, which was one of the initial attractions, works, but only marginally well, and the unit is unstable when laid down on a solid surface. If the mile-long charging cord is attached, expect to have to prop the unit against a wall to prevent it rolling over.
I spent $6.00 on a top-quality replacement cell for my T800 (shipping was $4.00, for a 700mA Sanyo AA), and after careful disassembly and reassembly, successfully replaced the ailing cell without breaking anything. Unbelievably, The T800 now outlasts the T970 two-fold.
I wish I'd stuck it out with the T800 in the first place.
.
Updated June 29, 2009.
After about two years of irregular use and the resulting regular complete self-discharges, the T970 stopped functioning even while plugged in, unless first given time to recharge. Initial time to start working after plugging in has increased to the point where it no longer works even being plugged in for 24 hours.

title: Different colors to fit just about every color out there. Powder blends still and gives a softer look than foundation and thick
text:
This contouring kit is so much fun! I've been playing with contouring a little over the past few months but was trying to piece everything together with random finds at the stores and as a beginner it was hard to pick the correct colors I need to do a true contoured look. Having this palette with so many choices makes it easy for me to play and perfect my look. The powder goes on well and the colors transfer great onto skin. Using powder instead of a heavier concealer or foundation gives it a Much softer look and not like I've caked a bunch of crap onto my face. They blend together well and last all day. They include an ebook tutorial on contouring tips and tricks in an email after purchase and I found it to be extremely helpful. There are also about a billion and one videos in YouTube that show you clearly step by step. I am much more confident in my makeup application now and can't believe how easy it is with the right tools. There is a good amount of powder of each color and I think this palette will last me a long time. There are many colors to fit just about every skin tone and color, a fantastic starter kit! I love it!

I did receive my contouring makeup palette free or at a promotional price for testing and evaluation purposes. I was not paid for my review and the was asked only to provide whatever feedback I may have regarding the product, whether it be positive or negative. My review is solely based on my experience and honest opinions of this palette.

title: Nice wig!
text:
I purchased this wig in black for a Halloween costume for work. I was planning on dressing up as Mia Wallace from Pulp Fiction. I had purchased a five dollar black wig from Walmart and it looked awful and had to be returned!! I decided I would give this a go, but at this price I didn't expect much. Boy, was I wrong!

This wig is a great quality! The product picture is an accurate representation of the actual product. The hair looks real and doesn't scream "fake". It held almost it's perfect shape right out of the bag. Couple of shakes and it was fine. It came with a hair net which was nice. The hair on the wig is very thick.No thin spots like you would find on cheap Halloween wigs. The bangs were not too long on me (I read from other reviewers they were too long and needed to be trimmed). However, the front pieces meant to scoop under the chin just came straight out and were kind of funny looking. They could have been easily adjusted with a little trimming.

In the end I decided not to wear the costume. This was in no way the wigs fault. No matter how much makeup I put on I looked absolutely awful in jet black hair. I'm about as fair a complexion as you can get, so I looked almost sickly in this color. However, at this price I might by a wig or two for fun in different, more complimenting colors in the future.

title: Great For Clean Up When The Area Is A Little Injured
text:
I normally use witch hazel for back door clean up. It helps sooth tissues & reduces uncomfortable problems. When clean up takes overly long due to pasty movements, the delicate tissue can get tiny tears that sting like nobodies business when cleaning up with witch hazel. This is where this cleansing lotion shines.

Admittedly, it is weird putting lotion in that particular location, but it does make pasty clean up more comfortable.

To assist healing & other minor back door problems, I recommend finishing up with WholeNest Less Than A Day - Hemorrhoid Relief Essential Oil Serum ( https://smile.amazon.com/gp/product/B0B7LYFBDR ). I was skeptical, but it does really work.

Regarding my Lady Garden, I haven't used this product for cleaning my Lady Garden. title: Aloe Vera Gel
text:
This Aloe Vera Gel by Sdara Skincare takes a few minutes to fully absorb into my skin. I worried it would leave behind a sticky feeling, since I could feel it on my arm the whole time it took to dry. But once the gel finally absorbed, I couldn’t feel any residue left behind. I didn’t notice much of any scent either, which is another positive for me.

The gel is clear; the plastic bottle is amber colored to help keep light out. I wish the bottle had a flip top or squeeze lid instead of a screw off cap.

The list of ingredients is what gives me pause. I’m not sure why sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) would need to be added to the organic aloe leaf juice. The product description mentions “we perfected our serum by enriching with Vitamin E, & Citric Acid for improved nutrient absorption.” I don’t see Vitamin E listed in the ingredients.

I will probably end up using this aloe vera gel to make hand sanitizer. The gel is thick, so it should be good for that use.

title: DO NOT BUY! WILL RIP YOUR HAIR OUT!
text:
DO NOT BUY THESE. They are absolutely horrible. The beads used are NOT silicone lined, and the metal will not open once closed. I would doubt they’re even using legit micro beads, just cheap metal. I’ve literally had to CUT them out of my head after the first track ended up ripping a chunk of my hair out.
I’ve worn many sets of extensions, micro beads, tape ins, EZ weft, sew in weft- all of which I buy regularly(about every 4 months) on amazon. I tried buying the EZ weft for the first time from LaaVoo, and I’m completely mortified.
You can see in the pictures, these are no more than 2 weeks old. My stylist placed the last track in the wrong spot and when she went to open the bead, it folded on top of itself and now it cannot be undone. I just experienced the SAME issue when opening the second bead on the track. I’m wearing this thing to GROW my hair, not rip it out!?!? Do not buy unless you want to go bald. HORRIBLE PRODUCT.

No I do not have that issue with any other brand. I also have never had my hands covered in residue just from a small scratch to the area. Smell was minimal, only stayed around for an hour or so which is fine, but definitely did not last 72 hours. After working out and reapplying after a shower it only lasted till the end of the day at most. I do not believe it's worth the money, works about as well as any other brand and not nearly as expensive so I will not be buying again.

title: Shaves as good as a blade - if you use it right
text:
This is the second 7775X I have owned. The first lasted two years, but this after extensive travel all over the world. It does shave as well as a blade (I use one of those too now and then) if you use it right (see below)

Positives

1) Quick charging - less than an hour to charge.
2) Long lasting - I have used it 10 days without recharging - great if you travel.
3) Seems to prevent ingrown hairs - which is something I run into when I use a blade consistently. My face definitely breaks out much less when I use this razor versus a blade (and I own some expensive blade razors as backup!).
4) So long as you follow instructions (don't press down hard, but when you need to go over an area again, do it firmly) you won't get nicked. Nice when you are in a strange hotel in a strange time zone and waking up jet lagged but needing to look presentable for a meeting.

Negatives

1) A little expensive
2) The shaving lotion cartridges are also expensive - I would just buy a can of regular shaving Gel (eg Edge).
3) Constantly pressing the dispenser for more gel can be a pain in the neck, and you go through those cartridges quickly (one every 10 days at most). If I wanted to redesign this shaver, I would junk the shaving dispensor entirely - I now find it just another moving part with the hinged lid.
4) I wish Norelco had come up with a way for this razor to use a standard charger jack of some kind, as having another electrical cord when I travel (especially one that does not work with multiple corded adaptors such as I-go) means I don't take the cord unless I am gone more than 8-10 days.

To use this right, just buy regular shaving gel, put it on your face, wait 30 seconds for the gel to soften your beard, and go for it. This is the best way I have found to get a clean, smooth shave. Gel in a can also has the advantage of going on thickly (versus the Coolskin dispensor) and not drying up quickly. Also, having a can of the shaving gel when you travel (checked baggage of course) can also come in handy if your Norelco runs out of charge, and you need to switch to that backup Gillette you should have in your shaving kit (I travel with enough cords as it is).

title: Perfect for my curly hair!
text:
A silk pillowcase isn't enough when you live in a dry climate with curly hair that is prone to frizz. It's exposed to the elements, with minimal protection. Regardless of the product you use, there are still a million other things a woman must contend with such as hormones, aging, etc., that don't help. I notice a huge difference with my hair when sleeping with a satin cap.

I definitely recommend for medium-thick, mid-length hair. After trying several caps, this is the best quality thus far. I love the size, and after several washes, the material is intact, and the caps still feel silky. I wash these in either the hand wash or delicate cycle in cold water in my machine. Hang dry.

What I don't like:
- The band of the cap around my head is slightly large, so wearing it as it doesn't work for me.
- I sleep with a sleep mask. The mask will press the lining of the band against my forehead, creating a god awful line that will last a good majority of the day.

Solution:
- Roll up the band once or twice. This prevents lines from forming on my forehead, and the cap stays in place much better.

title: Never going back to regular mascara again

After using this i believe that it is actually quicker and way easier than applying layer after layer of standard mascara to get big results. Application is a super simple it's a 3 step process and the kit has clear directions for application. The mascara that comes with the kit works alone to make the lashes look good for days your not feeling like getting huge lashes. The fibers are easy to apply and the wand for applying them picks up a great amount of fibers each time it is dipped in the tube. I found that using the 3d mascara fiber set is a cleaner process than layering mascara as well because there is far less clumping which in turn means less time brushing lashes and cleaning up the fragments that fall all over your freshly done makeup. Needless to say I will probably always chose a fiber lash over a standard mascara any day. The Grace & Stella 3d fiber lashes set arrives in a very classy decorative box which shows that the manufacturer takes pride in their product as well as makes storing this kit easy . This entire kit makes having thin short eyelashes not seem to bad after all and is definitely worth repurchasing.

title: Norelco is still the best buy for your money
text:
I've used this Norelco 9199XL for a month now and ......no surprises. It's still the best electric shaver you can buy. Even the less expensive Norelco shavers are the best no "frills" no nonsense and quietest electric shavers on the market.
Personally I enjoy some of the "frills". The usage by the minute and time remaining before charging, the adjustable "sensitive skin" shaving level makes a noticeable difference for me, the triple "floating head" system not only provides a smoother shave (smooth as a blade in my opinion) but a faster shave as well, and of course the time saving and efficient "rinse clean" feature for easier maintenance. The large multi-level case that store the shaver, charger and hard shell compact travel case along with numerous other goodies (including a collector 1/32 race car)are a bit over done and expensive. All very impressive for the special gift/collector kind of person. It's all in how much the "ultimate" is worth to you.

title: Poor design.
text: I am a lifelong user of Norelco electric razors (20+ years). I have 3 of them all working. I've had this one for close to 3 years and almost never use it. It shaves poorly, because it cannot be cleaned properly. On the older models you can remove and clean the individual blades. After a week of use, on the older models you can see they need thorough cleaning as residue accumulates at the cutting  edge. This can only be done with a brush, AFTER you remove the blade. The Arcitec doesn't allow you to do this. No amount of rinsing in the water will clean the Arcitec heads. Those who say "cleans easily" don't realize that rinsing the cutting head only cleans it superficially. Also as others noted the razor will not hold the whiskers when shaving and will make a mess. The chambers for clippings storage are ridiculously small and you need to empty them after each use. No matter how many passes you take, it will miss some hairs.

title: Great, but use before showering
text:
I bought this trimmer after my old Norelco stopped charging. For the record, I maintain a full beard with this. Overall, I am satisfied with the product, but there are some caveats.

Vacuum: Like me, you may be interested in the included vacuum, rather than getting hair all over the place. It works pretty well for this! I'd say about 90% of the hair is trapped in the canister, about 3% on the sink, and about 7% left in my beard. For this reason, I recommend using the trimmer before you shower. Otherwise, you will get little hairs all over your shirt during the day. If you only have a goatee or similar, it may not be quite as bad. It's not much hair, but the little vacuum can't solve everything.

Noise: The noise level is loud, but not really any noisier than an electric toothbrush. To me, it's really no big deal. With the bathroom door closed, you can only hear it when close to the door anyway. I even run it in turbo mode most of the time, and still don't have an issue with this. Of course it would be great if it were completely silent, but really it's not that bad.

Trimming: Like all Norelcos, it does a great job trimming. The included adapter(s) handle just about everything I could imagine needing. I am a hairy guy, and have not felt any pulling or ripping of the hairs, just a clean trim.

Power: I typically get several weeks worth of trimming (every 2-3 days) on a single charge. To maintain the battery, always ensure you fully drain it before recharging. Using turbo mode drains it slightly more quickly. I can go a whole vacation without needing to recharge, which is nice, but the included travel bag is handy just in case.

So, overall I'd give it 4.5 stars, only because of the leftover hairs. It would be nice to have a slightly more powerful vacuum, but I realize it's difficult to get the hairs that are trapped in a beard. I love the trimmer, and have not seen anything else out there that would beat it.

text:
To preface, I bought this product for when my hands or feet randomly get dry, and it seems I'll have to keep searching for a better product. Ultimately, I discarded this product. I do not recommend this because it did not moisturize my hands or feet. TIP: The best way to know if a product is good for your skin is to use it on your hands, feet, and/or face, since those are the most sensitive and picky areas (skin-wise) of the body. It dried out my hands and feet quickly after use, so I knew it would have the same effect on the rest of my body even if the effect is not as noticeable in other areas.

I tried to use this product when my hands or feet got dry. I like being in cold environments, so I often have lotion or something on standby for when my hands or feet inevitably get dry. Whenever I used this, my hands became dry again after about 30 minutes, when I expect it to last at least an hour like other products I've tried. So I decided this moisturizer is not for me and I ended up throwing this away.

Environmental considerations aside, I did not mind the glitter in the product. I just wish they focused more on the product actually working vs it looking pretty upon application.

title: Used for more than a dozen times. Here’s my tips and review.
text:
Okay so I have used this product more than a dozen times more than a year. I love it. Works great for my eye brows. Here is what I have learned:
1. It can stain your counters/sink. Put a towel or rag down. If you do get it on your counters clean immediately. Soft scrub works well!
2. It doesn’t stain your skin. Don’t worry about trying to be neat about it and being detailed to your eye brow. Just slap that crap all over and set your phone timer for however long you want for how dark you want (I prefer 3 mins for a dark brown)
3. Set up. Mixing in that little bowl can be messy. I like to use a Q tip to slowly mix it. This way it does not get everywhere on my brush and I make sure I don’t leave any unmixed powder on my brush when I apply to my face.
4. Clean up. Cleaning that bowl again don’t want to get on the counters when you have extra left in the bowl. I use a baby wipe to get the extra out before rinsing the bowl and brush. This way I make sure my counters stay unstained!

title: so it can get pretty hot down here
text: I live in the South, so it can get pretty hot down here. I also have some skin problems (mild to moderate acne, redness, dry flakes), so I like to wear base makeup whenever I go out. I bought this because I was hoping that combined with a primer and fixing spray, the skin prep pro would help my makeup stay put and not get swept away by the flood of sweat that comes crashing in every summer. The primer and fixing spray I use work well for me (or at least are the best I've tried so far) and sweat is still an issue for me. When this came in, I had high hopes, but unfortunately this is not a miracle product. It did make my face feel tight, so I applied a small amount of moisturizer over it after letting it set but before applying my makeup. I'm a college student, so I walk to class all the time, and after a few classes I could tell that my face was fading and melting as usual. It wasn't a pretty sight. So the next day, to see if it was due to the moisturizer, I just applied the skin prep pro before my makeup. Sadly, I got the same results. I break it out every now and again to see if it's a certain product that it won't work with, but after going through all of my routine taking one product out at a time, I've determined that this just doesn't work for me. I gave this two stars because while it didn't do what I wanted it to, it also didn't make any of my skin problems worse. I also want to give it the benefit of the doubt in case it is helping, just not significantly enough.

It will stay on if I am drinking something or even eating, but if wipe my mouth with a napkin it will come off. So a touch up after eating may be necessary. I don't need to use an oil or makeup remover to remove the lipstick, which is what I like.

title: Definitely not the results I was hoping for…
text:
I ordered this night cream as a form of TLC for my sometimes combination skin and although I was hoping to incorporate it into my routine (especially during the winter months when the temperature drops and tends to wreak havoc on my finicky skin.) I’m afraid that this one was a huge miss for me.

While I did enjoy the overall creaminess of this particular night cream and I definitely appreciated the fact that I didn’t have to use a lot to achieve maximum coverage, (a pea-sized amount on my fingertip did the trick.) I found the smell of this product to be a little heavier and lingering than you might expect of a product meant to be used before bed, but that I could bypass… what I couldn’t however, was that next day feeling.

Instead of waking up and feeling as if my skin were properly hydrated and moisturized, I found that the overall texture had become noticeably dryer than before I began to incorporate this product into my nighttime routine and that throughout several weeks I found myself having to apply creams and lotions throughout the day to help get my skin’s texture back to its natural state, which is why I can only give this one star.

⭐️

While it’s true that everyone experiences different results, if your skin tends to dry out easily, I’d recommend using extreme care when trying out this particular brand of night cream.

title: light, floral lavender
text:
Lavender is my #1 go-to essential oil. I think I use it in 90% of my blends, whether in the diffuser or in topical blends (or sprays, etc). The only thing I definitely don’t use it for is my home-made cleaning products where citrus oils are the only essential oils.

This particular oil is a very delicate scent – some lavender oils can pack quite a punch in terms of the scent. This is certainly floral and has a ‘high note’ to it. You can easily pick it out of a blend. It’s my first oil from New York Biology and I’m very pleased.

I love blending lavender in particular with chamomile, as it has an almost instantaneous calming effect. Also love to blend it with bergamot, ylang ylang, mandarin, and a drop of lime.

The bottle is very large, a dark brown color to protect the oil from UV rays which can damage the oils and decrease the shelf life. My favorite way to dispense oils is with the eye dropper (the euro droppers found in smaller bottles tend to gush several drops at once, so that’s definitely not my preference) – you can really control how many drops you dispense with these eye droppers.

Arrived well protected in bubble wrap and there was no leaks.

* I received the oil for evaluation and review

It looks like a white gel but when you apply it to the face it absorbs clear. It wasn’t sticky and it soothed her redness and itching.

The ingredients say it has aloe vera and all sorts of things but we were happy that there was no fragrance.

The tube is a good size (2 ounces) but she was using it on her face and all over her back so it ran out pretty quickly.

We would recommend this product to anyone with irritated or sensitive skin.

title: Sweet, lovely, smells just like the name
text:
I am not an impulse buyer. So before I spend $$$ on a parfum, I did some extensive research. First, I knew I wanted to have a Tom Ford because of his reputation in making parfum. Then I narrowed down the choices to the 5 most preferred private blends by online reviewers. Then I bought .5ml sample for each. After trying them one by one, my husband made the choice for me: Noir de Noir, but my favorite is Amber Absolute.

Vanilla Tobacco is really sweet and nice, with straight forward vanilla tabacco notes. Vanilla is sweet and yummy. The tabacco smells like Turkish tobacco: sweet and sexy. I can imagine that whoever wears this could be quite irresistible. This is one of my favorite. However, compared to Noir de Noir, I feel that it lacks a bit complexity. It is a bit straight forward Vanilla and tobacco.

From my research and trial, I find that parfum is very personal, and you have to try it in person. It is better to try it at home and wear it once or twice before you buy it at a store. The 5 scents I tried:

- Noir de Noir: complex, sweet, lovely scent. Not overwhelmingly floral, not overpowering, and not sharp/edgy. This is my husband's favorite when I wear it.

- Vanilla Tobacco

- Amber Absolute: Unique, herbal. Stronger than Noir de Noir. Initially, I couldn't stand Amber Absolute. It smelled to me like Chinese medicine box. But then I am totally in love with it. It is very unusual. It has a very unique dried herb fragrance and very complex. It reminds me of the fragrance in the air in Big Sur, CA or Mono Lake, CA when the flowers and plants are drying in fall. The fragrance is not floral, not particularly sexy or feminine.

- Tuscan leather: couldn't stand it, very leathery.

- Neroli Portofino: didn't like it at all. Just like some reviewers said, it smells like Tide.

title: Excellent Quality All Natural Ingredients
text:
I really like these. Unsure why my lips get chapped, perhaps I talk way too much 😉

Locally, I'll purchase Burt's Bees or whatever natural products are available. Online lends vast selections with potential skepticism about quality. These were great because they were all unique in scent/taste of essential oils and yet all the same composition between sticks. I really like Lavender, as it's relaxing and mentally can be a cognitive clearer before bed. The Cinnamon Orange is an excellent combination as I like the combination of smell and flavor. Peppermint a classic scent always enjoyed and like the quality of the oils they use.

Ingredients, as listed, are simply Coconut Oil, Shea Butter, Sweet Almond Oil, Beeswax, Vitamin E and essential oils. These are the same types of ingredients I'd used when attempting to craft my own lip balms. It's fun to make them at home and yet with inexpensive prepackaged offerings like this readily available, it becomes unnecessary to create the mess when great ones like this are merely a quick mouse click away.

They come in a nice box with all four smells/flavors (one is totally unscented, just a slight pleasant aroma from the shea butter and almond oil, I really like this one too). It would work nicely as a gift. I like how the different tubes are different colors for the different essential oils used, so I don't have to look and read each one as the composition of materials each look the same (orange doesn't look orange as they don't use extra ingredients) and it's easy to switch between them. As mentioned, I like the cinnamon/orange or peppermint during the day for the aromatherapeutic benefits and passionate about all things Lavender at nighttime.

title: excellent product!
text: This is an excellent product.  I've been using this for almost a year now, and will continue to buy it.  I have read the other two reviews, and my experience with it is far more favorable.  I was looking for a fluid, non oily sunscreen to use under my mineral makeup or under my Laura Mercier tinted moisturizer.  This turned out to be perfect.  It has a milky consistency (not oily) and absorbs almost immediately and leaves a smooth finish (not sticky or oily).  It does have a brightening effect after application.  Any kind of makeup can be applied over it.  It has SPF 40, which is very difficult to find. And the sunscreen lasts all day long.  I never ever burn after applying this, even when I've been outside in the sun for hours.  I will agree that the pump doesn't work great (hence the 4 stars).  I've learned to just press the pump lightly into the palm of my hand, to reduce the odds of it spraying or streaming out irregularly.  But that doesn't keep me from wanting to continue using it.  One small bottle also lasts a long time (like 6 months or more)... because a little goes a long way.

title: Looks delicious and smells great!
text:
After years of mistreating my hair with hair color products and a bleaching mishap a month ago, I was looking for something to re-fortify my hair while conditioning and giving me extra moisture. So I bought the Hairgurt Intense Repair Deep Conditioner in Almond honey. Let me just tell you that it really does look edible!! And it smells edible! It's not a strong smell and it leaves no trace of scent afterwards. The texture is also that of food--it looks and feels like whipped butter.
But we don't buy this to eat it so how did my hair do? My hair did very well but not better than with other less costly products. That is why this gets 4 stars instead of 5. My hair is normally wavy but is not coarse. Due to hair coloring as I mentioned, it had taken a beating and my wave actually diminished but my hair became coarser and very frizzy. After using this hairgurt the first day, my hair did feel smooth afterward for the rest of the day. It dried a bit frizzy but not much. I do not blow dry my hair. However, after going to bed at night, sleeping on cotton pillowcase, my hair was super coarse and frizzy the next day. I did not give up. I used the treatment for 3 more days but always with the same result. I've had better results with other treatments I bought at a local beauty supply store. However the hairgurt DOES work, if only for the day. Maybe it will be better with continued use and if I continue and get better results, I will then update my review.
Please ask any questions and I will do my best to answer. Thank you.

title: Roll on Wax Bar
text:
The wax warmer is very color pleasing and so are the bars of wax that go inside the warmer itself. It comes with two roll on wax bars, along with finishing wipes (just oil towel) and a pack of paper. The directions are very easy to read on how to use the wax warmer but the pack of papers is awful in the direction department. So many spelling/grammar errors - but the pictures are more explanatory then the actual words themselves.

It took about 5-8 minutes for the wax to melt and be at a good use of temperature. I would wait until MAX temperature and then wait a few more minutes for the wax to go on smoother. Then slap the paper on.

Now, you are wondering why 3 stars?

1. The time it takes to warm up - 1 Star
2. The materials that are given plus directions - 2 Star

If you are doing a spot on shave sure go ahead but to do your entire leg and not to mention BOTH of them, you will be sitting for a very long time. Its too time consuming and I am impatience. I would suggest to buy more papers if you are thinking about getting this. I do feel that it is a learning curve and that is ok, I will continue to learn how to do it.

It does work though, works decently well once the wax is at the perfect temperature. Oh its not painless, but it is tolerable if you have a high pain tolerance, but if you DO NOT, I would suggest waxing to not be your thing.

text:
why use a primer... well below are some reasons why you must:)

I loved this one because you need very little to make your face looks all smooth and fresh.

read what else I found:
If you haven’t been using makeup primer, then you’re really missing out.

Whether you wear a full face of makeup everyday or you prefer to keep it simple with BB cream and tinted lip balm, makeup primer is a godsend when trying to combat shine or create a smooth base for foundation.

Yet, many people often skip this step because they believe they don’t really need it, are unsure of how to use it or don’t think it’s worth the investment. However, using primer underneath your makeup will make everything last longer, whether that’s on your skin or in your cosmetics bag. We asked two beauty experts to discuss the ins and outs of primer and why it’s a must-have. Here’s the deal.

Makeup primer is a base for foundation or face makeup that allows it to go on smoother and last longer.

Formulas are available in cream, gel and powder. While moisturizer softens skin, editorial and celebrity makeup artist Stephen Dimmick points out that primer prepares skin for your favorite tinted moisturizer or foundation, giving it something to hold onto so it lasts throughout the day.

Many makeup primers are formulated with silicone-based polymers like dimethicone because of their ultra-smoothing effects. But for those who struggle with cystic acne and other skin conditions, do a patch test or check with a medical professional before use, as there have been reports of allergic reactions to dimethicone.

Physicians Formula’s celebrity makeup artist Joanna Schlip adds that makeup primers can also help smooth any fine lines, wrinkles or large pores. If you are searching for a face primer to help color-correct rosacea or conceal acne spots, Schlip’s go-to is this light green primer as that hue cancels out redness.

Primers are even available just for your eyelids to prevent creasing. “I’d recommend pairing a primer with extra long-wear [eyeshadow or eyeliner] formulas for a look that will really stay put,” says Schlip. If you have oilier eylids, Dimmick advises that you use a mattifying eyelid primer.

Using your fingertips to apply makeup primer allows you to really work the product into your skin.

Schlip thinks it’s best to apply primer with fingertips for areas larger than a quarter. “If only using for small areas, I’d recommend a brush. I like to use a concealer or lip brush for this,” she said. This spot-treat technique gives the look of a more natural finish, according to Schlip.

Taking this routine a step further, Dimmick considers the process “an exercise for the face.” He explains that massaging primer onto skin with fingertips helps “to get the blood flowing underneath.” We’ll consider that the next time we’re meditating while putting on makeup.

Primer is a life-saver for getting your makeup to stay-put in extreme weather.

If you live, visit or plan on traveling anywhere that experiences hot and humid or cold and dry temperatures, Schlip suggests having primer handy to help your makeup wear longer. However, Dimmick advocates for using primer year-round. “No matter what the weather is like outside, this extra step will save you a lot of time and effort.”

Primer can even be worn on its own.
Love the fresh no-makeup look but want to look a bit more polished than when you first woke up? Primer is your answer. The product can be worn on your eyelids and double as a light eyeshadow, or it can serve as a lighter alternative to foundation.

title: I bought this why...?
text:
The only reason this razor works--and the only reason I rated it with even 3 stars--is because it's a Venus razor. Venus razors work excellently, so when this new version came out I was excited to try it. I've owned mine for over a year now, and was unimpressed with it from the moment I started using it.

The vibrations are useless; I get the same quality shave whether it's turned on or off. The battery lasted quite a long time, but as soon as I replaced it the razor stopped working properly (not that it mattered, since it didn't do anything in the first place). So not only do we have a "power" razor that has no power, but the company decided to charge even more for replacement razor cartridges. I thought I had to use the corresponding cartridges, but finally I inserted one of the old Venus cartridge heads and it worked exactly the same.

I don't know--maybe I'm just a no-fuss girl who really doesn't notice subtle differences in the quality of leg-shaving. I would recommend Venus brand razors in a heartbeat, but I do NOT recommend this particular version.

title: Get it!
text:
I have been using the Helen of Troy Brush Iron for twenty years. Recently, the one I owned for 10+ years finally died. I tried to see if there was something better out there thinking something newfangled would be better. I hunted online for hrs and finally found a more expensive tourmaline model by Conair. Hated it. It's going back. Unlike my trusty Helen of Troy, it was scorching to the touch and easy to burn myself. Unlike my Helen of Troy, it was hard to get my hair to hold to it so I had to wear expensive protective gloves to try to hold my hair to the hot tool. Wearing those gloves made it impossible to section my hair with my fingernail to curl my thick hair all over. The tourmaline model was complicated, painful, more time-consuming and less effective.

Helen of Troy has three settings: High, Low, Off. I set mine on high and it is just right. It gives a very natural full-bodied polished look to hair. I have a lot of fine, straight hair in addition to some curly thick grays. This tool tames and styles them all for a very cohesive polished look. It gently curls the ends under or over with no unsightly crimp on them like a regular curling iron. One of the only shortcomings of the H of T model is that it takes about 5 minutes for it to fully heat up. I heat mine up before I go in shower. When I come out I use mousse in my hair, dry it with my negative Ion hairdryer and give a gentle curl with my trusty Helen of Troy afterwards. It only takes me about 6 minutes to curl my thick straight shoulder length hair. While looking for an alternative tool from my trusty Troy I read that some styling tools take 30-60 minutes to style hair. Personally, I have better things to do than spend that much time in front of a mirror each day. I have had many compliments on my hair over the years. THE EFFECT IS MUCH LIKE AN EXPENSIVE BLOW OUT. The bonus is you don't have to go to the salon to get it. Sometimes I look at Kate Middleton and think I could do a better job on her hair with this trusty inexpensive tool.

Look no further. Just get it.

title: Awesome!
text: Ok, I wanted to wait before I did a review on this product because I literally just got my labret done and it's not even healed. But the ball fell off of my starter jewelry and fell down the drain in my shower and these pieces were the only ones I had to replace it with unless I wanted to let it close up. I will admit it was hard to get on, the studs are very dainty and I kept dropping them while trying to align them with the post while I held it against my lip. I'm not sure how much of that tho was because my piercing wasn't ready to change jewelry and it was still slightly swollen. Well, after I got it in I haven't taken it out and am letting my piercing heal with this piece of jewelry. I paid attention to the back of my lip, the sides of the post are a bit sharp and for the first day or two I had begun to worry that the acrylic post was doing more than just 'nesting'. I was worried it was cutting the back of my lip. Ladies and gents, this fear has subsided and I am having a totally normal reaction to this jewelry. After a week I decided I absolutely LOVED these pieces because the post doesn't feel like it's eroding my enamel or my gums, and I think you literally have to be pulling on your jewelry and twisting it aggressively for it to really tear up the back of your lip. After my swelling has gone down I've realized the posts for these pieces are short enough for me to practically never bite them when I'm chewing and long enough for my lip to not feel in any kind of danger. The studs are sturdy and I really don't think they'll fall out. Only thing I'm sad about is that I lost the clear one in my haste to put another piece in my unhealed piercing. But like I said, I think it was only hard to get on because mine was new and it was quite painful to change it. But tbh my piercing has been healing completely fine with these in it!

text: This STUFF ROCKS! I've been using this serum since April 8th,  2017- everyday and my skin has never felt or looked better. Seriously, I've suffered from a mild case of Rosacea now for the last year after going through menopause and it seemed like I was going backwards to the days when I was a teenager and acne was my problem then. Since having the rosacea, and the fact that it really messes with my self-esteem, even at 53 yrs old- I've been looking desperately and trying lots of creams, supplements, to smearing honey on my face (which tastes great, but sticky!) to cutting out red wine and avoiding spicy foods long before trying this serum and nothing helped until NOW. I'm sorry I don't have any before and after photos, but I definitely would recommend this product.regardless of your skin issues. It's gentle and if I was going to break out from it, I definitely would have by this time and thrown it in the wastebasket, but I didn't and it seems to have reduced my redness and the red bumps on the sides of nose have also noticeably been reduced and smoother skin is there now.

title: GREAT for easily crafting DIY salicylic acid products; misleading review below
text:
The only Salicylic Acid active ingredient product you need to effectively and easily create your own DIY Salicylic Acid skin care products! Not intended to be used as is (as it states in the description... faulty review below).

This MakingCosmetics product in particular is superior to any other Salicylic Acid active ingredient I've ever seen. This is because of the fact that this is a PRE-DISOLVED SOLUTION and not straight Salicylic Acid powder. This possesses many benefits...

(1) You don't run into any solubility issues combining SA into your base/cosmetic preparation
(2) This ensures maximum cosmetic benefits and effectiveness of the SA to the skin
(3) Makes getting a precise concentration of SA very simple (5% of the liquid solution = 2% active SA...)
(4) High solubility with water based ingredients (like a Dickinson's Witch Hazel Toner, etc) makes adding the SA to a finished/storebought cosmetic product easy

I use this item by adding it in 5% concentration (2% active BHA) to the MakingCosmetics Skin Toner Base or to straight witch hazel water. Dramatically helps with smoothing irregular tone/texture of my skin, decreases excess sebum production, treats acne breakouts/blackheads.

Easy way to incorporate BHA into your skincare regimen, while avoiding all the unnecessary fillers/fluff/harshness of storebought BHA products.

JUST BE SURE to purchase pH testing stripes (0-6 range) and Triethanolomine when using this product. For SA to be effective, the overall pH of the finished recipe needs to be MANUALLY CHECKED AND ADJUSTED to around 3.5.

title: Great for an electric, but still doesn't compare to fusion razor
text:
I gave this to my husband, hoping he would get hooked by even the novelty of it and use it everyday, I like a freshly clean shaven look on him and he had been slacking lately using his fusion disposables.

He's tried a few electric razors in the past but was never happy with them so has stuck to using the upper end disposables.

His take: very comfortable to hold, the heads conform to his face well and it's a comfortable shave... as long as it's very lubricated. He found he got the best feeling and closest share only when he used a lot of shaving creme/gel & had his face very well, making for quite a mess on the sink top. He liked the idea of the dispensing lotion but found he preferred his normal shaving gel and also didn't think the dispenser held enough or that it was cost effective. On the upside this is water proof so I'll get him to try it in the shower next. He also agrees with the other reviewers who said it's just not a close enough shave, and that it takes far to long. The whole idea of a electric is to speed up the process but between loading it with the conditioner, using it, adding more water, cleaning up the mess etc it was taking him 2x as long to shave at least, and with less steller results.

My take: I think his face looks better, the shave may not be as close however there are no knicks or aggrivation and his skin looks better all around. Of course I see his point on the time consuming aspect.

The booklet recommends giving it 3 weeks, I'm not sure I'll be able to talk him into that... wonder how this thing would work on my legs? ;)

title: Helped my recurring chalazion and stye problem
text:
I’m writing this review for people like me who struggle with recurring chalazions and styes:
I started getting chalazion 5 years ago on both eyes and I don’t know what even started it. It was very frustrating because these bumps would form on my eyelid and nothing worked on them. I got surgery once to get rid of one on my lower eye lid. They stayed for months and warm compresses and eye massages that doctors recommended me did nothing.
I read many research papers to see what I can do to help my condition and here is a summary:
1. this eye wash helped so much. Since I started using this eye wash two months ago my chalazions are almost gone and very unnoticeable. I wash my eyes every mornings and before going to bed and also whenever it starts to itch.
2. One doctor prescribed an antibiotic called “Doxycycline” I think that also helped my condition
3. I make tea with tea leaves and I let it cool, then I use a cotton ball to put the tea inside my eyes. I use 3-4 drops in each eye.
4. mix of one drop of tea tree oil and 4 drops of olive oil and put it on your eye lid. I wash it after 15 mins.

That’s a summary. Some people say warm compresses help them but for me, I did not see noticeable results. I used to wash my eyes with baby shampoo and it worsened my condition. only this eye wash helped. My condition has improved 80% since I took the steps I mentioned.

title: Very effective, but terrible battery
text:
This came as promised, and I was happy when I opened it up. It has three barrels all together, with two different amounts of grit. The directions said to charge it for 8 hours before use, and that the light would turn green. When I plugged it in, the light was already green, which was a fun surprise.

This little machine is pretty powerful, and I love that it has 180° of use around the top, so I don't have to worry about flipping it around my foot.

My biggest problem with it is that it will only run for less than five minutes before it slows down to the point of it being unusable. I love that it's rechargable, because I don't have to flip out batteries again and again, but the very short life off the cord is a major drawback for me.

text: I cannot even begin to describe how much I love what this product does to my hair.  I use it with the Camille Rose Curl Love Moisturiser and my hair responds like a dream. title: Love love love!
text:
So I am super sweaty and tend to stink by the end of the day and I don't do much because I'm unable to work due to medical issues. I've tried many deodorants even men's and prescription and nothing helped. I then started to worry about breast cancer because of how far down I was having to put deodorant. I tried probably 5 natural deodorant and none of them worked! I smelled horrible! Even after detoxing my pits a few times it was still bad. Then I found this product. WOW! I don't stink at all! And I even feel like I'm sweating less! I love this so much! I got lavender and lemon. It does go on super wet! So make sure you give it time to dry before dressing. I have found no stains from it. Will buy over and over again!
*It's great to detox when switching to natural deodorant to get chemicals out that may be causing you to stink and sweat more (they can do that). Try using apple cider vinegar mixed with bentonite clay to make a paste. Start with 5 min or less on underarms and wash off. Then apply some aloe gel. Can extend time after a couple uses. Do it a few times the first week but don't over do it. Then 1x a month for a few.

title: I Just Had The Best Dental Checkup Of My Life!
text: I've been using my Oral B Vitality toothbrush for just over 6 months. I just had my 1st dental exam after using it, & it was the best ever! My dentist said my gums have never looked better, I didn't have to have ANY cleaning at all!! They just did my exam & polished my teeth. When I asked about the cleaning, the hygienist said I didn't need any cleaning because I did not have anything to be cleaned off! WOW! I love how the heads on the brush have bristles that stick out further on the outer row to get your gumline & between your teeth cleaned. It also has a 2 minute timer which doesn't reset itself if you stop for a few seconds to spit. I love this toothbrush & I hope Oral B never stops carrying it. I started using it after my previous brand's heads became harder & harder to find, & the price kept going up to almost $8/head! I called Oral B customer service & was referred to this brush. The rep said that this brush head only goes around & doesn't pulsate, so it's easier on your teeth. I had used a more expensive Oral B model for a few years, but it was just too hard on my teeth, & they became super sensitive. That's not the case with the brush. I love it! The Amazon price can't be beat either.

title: I have questions
text: I think this may have synthetic hair mixed in.  I used my flat iron on the hair at 380 degrees as I normally would on my hair and or extension hair and it seems to have burned some strands making the texture feel very wirey and rough.  I've worn extensions for years and years so I know what to expect and how to treat the hair. This was only after about 2 days of having it in.  I see now it says don't use heat over 180 and don't blow dry it which leads me to believe this cant be 100 Remy human hair.  Human hair extensions can be treated gently like your normal hair with out causing this kind of damage. I went through and trimmed it and deep conditioned which helped a little but overall it feels stiff and dry especially on the ends and upwards 4 or 5 inches.  Its kind of suspicious that 20 inch was under $60.  I'm super sad because its a perfect color match.

title: Very effective Body Wash
text: This is an excellent natural body wash that is perfect for anyone that has sensitive skin. I have been using this product for several weeks now and I am very surprised at how soft and moisturized my skin feels. I also used it as a conditioner for my hair and was shocked at how soft and revitalized my skin felt. The ingredients in this body wash prompted me to use this product on my toddler and I am happy to state that the results were excellent. The body wash left my daughters skin soft and not irritated, which has occurred in the past with popular body washes. This product is made up of Tea Tree Oil, Coconut Oil and Argan Oil. I am not sure if anyone knows this, but another name for Tea Tree Oil is melaleuca. Melaleuca is known to kill bacteria, fungus and viruses. This is a very powerful anti-bacterial body wash that is absolutely worth the price.

title: Fades, but very vibrant color
text:
This color is pretty good for the price. There are a few pros and cons to it that I will explain below, but overall I don't think I will buy it again.

Pro:
Comes with 2 containers which is enough color for any length hair
The ability to adjust the shade you want by adding conditioner to the color
A little bit goes a VERY long way
Price is very reasonable
When it fades, it fades all the way out so it doesn't look like some weird shade of orange when it starts to wash out
You don't need any heat to get the color to stick
It doesn't smell like anything

Cons:
Fades INCREDIBLY fast - I had it in for maybe a week or two before it washed all the way out
You have to use a ton of conditioner to create a light soft pink color - the size of a dime with about 2 cups of conditioner do the trick
It will dye everything it comes in contact with - use thick gloves
If you are diluting the color with conditioner, it will take some time to sit on your hair
If you are diluting the color, you'll never need both bottles - it would take 5 years to use all of it

I think overtone color conditioner is an easier system to use and the color stays longer.

title: This cream works for stretch marks!
text:
Stretch marks occur when skin is abnormally stretched and expanded for a period of time. The abnormal stretching causes the skin's support structure of collagen and elastin to break down or rupture. The visible stretch marks on the skin are actually bands of elastin that have broken inside and out of the skin. The skin can not get back to its original form quickly.

Stretch marks are among the toughest skin-care concerns to treat ... No matter how good a product is, change takes time ... you can't see results immediately. It takes commitment to apply stretch mark treatments two to three times daily over several months.

As for this cream, it is smooth to apply and is absorbed quickly by the skin. It is not greasy either.
I am losing weight and I can see a few silver lines on my inner arms. I rub a small amount of the product on my silver stretch marks in circular motions twice a day, once in the morning and once before bed. After a week they are visible only if I look for them now. This cream does lighten the mark to negligible and excellent for preventing further stretch marks.

I received this product in exchange for a fair and honest review!!

title: Horrible in every way, only for super sensitive skin. Will not give you exfoliation or deep pore cleansing
text: Four years ago I bought a generic version of the Clarisonic called the Nutrasonic. My little Nutrasonic quickly became my favorite four speed facial powerhouse. I loved my Nutrasonic (and still do) but sadly the company has decided to discontinue the brushes so my favorite little machine is no more. Hence I started shopping for a replacement and as before when I bought my Nutrasonic I was dead set on not getting a Clarisonic because of the high price of the machine. I came across the Philips Pure Radiance on Costco’s website and decided to order it thinking I would be happy with it since not only Costco but Amazon has rave reviews for the machine. I got the Philips yesterday and let me tell you this facial brush sucks big time. If you are used the battery operated facial brushes they sell at Wal-Mart, then you may like this weak inadequate machine, but if you are used to a powerhouse cleaner and exfoliater you will absolutely hate this machine as I did. First off, the Philips has only two speeds which are pretty slow. The brushes are super soft (even the supposed stiff pore cleansing brush) and the annoying timer that turns off the machine every 20 seconds to apparently remind stupid people to move to a different spot.  This machine was so weak and truthfully, I hated everything about this cleansing system as it did not leave my skin feeling clean what so ever. If you have super sensitive skin then perhaps this machine will be okay for you but those desiring a pore cleansing will be greatly disappointed. Beyond how weak the machine is in hindsight I should have thought about my current dilemma before ordering the Philips and that being the long term availability of brushes. Since facial cleansing systems are not Philip’s main seller I can guarantee that this machine will be like my Nutrasonic in a few years and have no brushes available. Sadly there is nothing nice I can say about this machine and can only say that if you are used to the Wal-Mart type of facial cleaners and not a Clarisonic then you will probably like this, but otherwise if you are used to the deep cleaning of a quality facial system you will certainly hate this machine. Of course I am taking this one back and have broken down to buying a Clarisonic for two reasons, the power of the machine and the future availability of the brushes. I can’t wait to get my new 4 speed Clarisonic Pro that I ordered off of E-Bay that cost me the same price as this Phillips did but will actually clean my acne prone aging skin.

title: Norelco finally got it right!
text:
I have used electric razors for over 35 years all have good points and all have weaknesses. Norelco razors have two problems.

Hard to clean. As the cutter and blades fill up the motor is not able to maintain the speed and nicks result. You must take the blade and cutter assembly apart to clean properly and if you cut longer hairs by accident(side burn area etc.) it will wrap around the cutting blade and really slow the motor down. The motor slowing is the worst on the new "3 blade" called the Speed XL, they just seem to be underpowered.(just as a note the older Spectra blade replacements fit these razors and seem to work better). Think of this problem like a paper shredder that has not beeen emptied and hasn't room for more papers in the bin.

Hidden Build up. I have changed batteries in several of my Norelco shavers and the build up of gunk where only disassembly could find and cure it, is remarkable(same with most brands). I suspect this is gunk is high in bacteria and the cause of most skin infections from nicks. The stuff you never knew was there is nasty!

Now for the cure: Norelco 8060x! This razor blade assembly has open backs for the stubble to fall through as you shave. I use mine in the shower so no mess. The blade assembly is completely seperate from the motor unit= no place for gunk to build up! The unit is entirely water proof. The blades disassemble easily so you can get at those occasional "longer hairs" that wrap around the cutters. The cutters have more blades on each, so the shave is faster and the motor seems stronger than previous models, hence less slowing. The Nivea solution is great, but you can use regular shave cream or gel. I like the Nivea Gel for Sensitive Skin, it seems to be the right thickness for the razor and works alomost as well as the cream you load in the razor.

The only negative for some people might be the size or shape of the handle, it is smaleer and lighter than most shavers. Some people with larger hands may find is inconvient and others may find it easy to drop.

Overall this is the best all around razor I have owned, there is a brief time required for your face to get used to any new razor... This ones worth it!!

title: Has the tools needed to handle our face/hands/feet/skin issues.
text:
Short summary: I like a number of the instruments available in this set; they work well for my needs, and for the needs of our family members.
The case securely holds each instrument in its slot; but some instruments are tough to remove from their slots.

More details:

a) Case is soft to the touch and comfortable to hold. There is a tab that I press in to open the case; to close the case, I press both parts of the case together.

b) One compartment (the side with the tab) holds:
--- Nail File
Works well to smooth out my nails after I trimmed them.
--- Fingernail Clipper
The curved blades cut my nails well. The lever has square-shaped divots that give good
grip. It is a bit hard to rotate the lever around the pin; but, with continued use, it should
be easier to open and close the lever. There is no nail file attached to the clipper
--- Nose trimmer
I did not find this to be as good at cutting my nose hairs.
---.Cuticle nipper
This trims my cuticles well.
--- Eyebrow trimmer
This does a decent job in trimming eyebrow
--- Cuspidal/Obtuse push-type broach
I didn't have a need to use this; but several family members used this, and they say it
does an adequate job with their nail polish routine
--- Ear pick
I tried it, but I didn't like the sensation of it scraping against the inside of my ear.

c) The other compartment holds:
--- Toenail clipper
Curved blades cut my toenails well; however, it does not cut my big toenail because
that nail has too much fungus and is too thick. I used a different toenail clipper
recommended by my foot doctor.
--- Oblique nail clipper
I don't use this often for cutting my nail; but I like how easily it cuts the nails. Other
family members love it, and are more familiar in using it
--- Nail cleaning knife
I don't use this; others in my family say it works well to remove the debris under their nails.
--- Tweezers
My family members do not like this, because it doesn't open enough for them to use; so they went
back to using their preferred tweezers
--- Zig Zag callus remover
--- Oblique callus remover
--- Flat callus remover
note: so far, those of us with calluses (including me) do not have them so thick that
they need removal using these instruments.
--- Dead skin fork
a family member used it; she says it is quite useful in removing the loose skin right
around her cuticle area
--- Ear pick (has something like a serrated shape, perhaps)
I much prefer this ear pick; it felt more natural and is like what I used in the past.
--- Acne tool (one end with a circular loop; other end with a pick)
--- Acne tool (one end with a circular loop; other end with a scoop)
None of our family members currently have acne, so we haven't had a chance to try it;
but, we have similar instruments just like these, and those worked well when we've
needed to deal with our skin issues.

Lastly: I would have liked the case to be slightly larger, so that it is easier to remove all these instruments. When I removed all the instruments, some of which I used, others which my family members used, and then put the instruments back into the case, we couldn't get it to close. Finally, we removed the toenail clippers, and that easily closed the case.

Overall, this is a good manicure set, with instruments that I and/or my family members find to be very useful for our particular needs.

title: Phenomenal!
text: I've tried many sheet masks, but this one is a standout. I was looking for pearl as an ingredient in a sheet mask, and it's hard to find.  I read a lot of reviews before purchasing.  It is loaded with essence (many of them are), but these masks are different in their results.  I honestly see the little fine lines under my eyes gone after using them.  They stay gone the next day or so, too.  I know, that sounds hard to believe, but for me, it's true. And there is some brightening effect, too, and definitely calmed redness and... spider veins.  Your results may vary, but it wouldn't hurt to try these masks if you've developed broken capillaries.  Certainly there is "plumping", and my skin is bouncy and supple the next morning. I will say that after removing the mask and patting, the essence stays a bit tacky, but with these results I don't mind.  Essence that remains in the packet I use the next night as one of my serum steps.  I certainly will reorder.

title: Not for dry skin
text: The first thing I noticed when I used the product was the delicate scent. It's very nice and stays on you for awhile. So, you pump out the 'gel' which I would call soap except for the fact that it doesn't lather much. Instructions say to pump in your hand then apply to face. I tried that, but my face felt too dry, so I moistened it a little bit and then applied the gel. I rinsed it off well and patted my skin dry. Within seconds I felt my skin drying up. If you have really dry skin, you know what I mean - that taut feeling you try to avoid. I had to get some cream and moisturize right away. I'm guessing there must be something like alcohol in this makeup remover. If you have oily skin or perhaps you're trying to remove a heavy foundation - this may be a good choice for you. But since I wear light makeup and have very dry skin, I can't use this. I'll try it again in the summer when my skin produces more oil as the scent is really nice and makes it worth a second try.

title: Hard to separate
text:
Review for Specific Beauty Skin Brightening Pads, Skin Smoothing for more Even Texture & Tone- Dark Spot Fading Kojic Acid & Licorice 90 X 2 180 Pads

I received on jar of skin brightening pads. Sometimes only partial orders are received by reviewers in the Vine program so I was not concerned with that. However, there is another reviewer that also only received one jar so I felt that I should mention that.

My jar arrived with a security seal beneath the lid. Once removed, I was able to pull out a few pads. I found it difficult to separate them so that I only got one to use on my face. It turns out that every time I use these, I have to fight to get them apart and make sure I am only using one.

Once I have separated out one pad, I like how they work. They are quick to use and they do a nice job on my skin. My skin has a tingling sensation when it comes in contact with the pads. This quickly passes. I use the pads after washing my face and after use, I apply a serum. My skin feels smooth and soft.

I'm glad that I did, because this polish is great. Aside from the fact that the glitter is very messy, this polish is very nice. It is very smooth and hardens quickly, as well. Whenever I went to remove the polish, I could glitter everywhere. I kinda saw it coming, but it was still a little bit annoying. This polish does last quite a long time, though.

title: Powerful and light, but tough to reach the inside spots!
text:
It’s compact, light, easy to charge and the tip isn’t too pointy. Well it’s pointy but it’s not a hook so I initially was afraid but as I used it, I realized it wasn’t going to stab my gums.

When I turned it on, I was surprised at how powerful the little tip was sonicating. I found it easy to use on the outside of my teeth, but it was awkward to get to the inside of my teeth. I think I will have to practice holding it differently, maybe. I bought this because I build up tartar on the inside of my bottom front teeth—directly where my tongue rests. I did mh best to clean there but saw only mild results after one use.

I will use it a couple more times and see if maybe I see better results and hopefully I will get used to how to hold it so I can access the inside of my teeth.

Now if someone did this for me, I think this little tool would work wonders!! It seems quite powerful. I will keep trying and update my return if I get better results.

title: High-Quality Synthetic Hair Extension! Will Buy Again!
text:
I was immediately enchanted with this hairpiece from the second I tried it on. I've tried wearing it a few days in a row now, and I'm not tired of it yet. First, I'll start by saying that I ordered it as an add-on item in "dark blue to peacock green." With Prime, it only took two days to get to my house; very fast and convenient shipping, and the hairpiece was encased in a clear plastic bag; I threw that away, as I keep my extensions in a silk hair case. This hairpiece is actually pretty high quality for synthetic, eccentric hair, so I would suggest something like that to keep it safe and untangled when you're not wearing it.

In my opinion, the product images do not really do this hairpiece justice. I can't say anything for other colors yet, but the dark blue to peacock green is pretty true to the product images, with sapphire blue roots and aqua-blue tips (although I will say the hairpiece I received is not half-and-half blue and green like the picture; mine is like an ombre gradient, approximately 2/3 blue and 1/3 aqua tips).

Many of the reviews here also have mixed comments on the thickness of the hairpiece; maybe the people who say it is "thin" are comparing it to their own natural hair? I will say that this piece is very thick for a single-weft hair extension. A weft is like a curtain of hair with a seam holding it all together at the top. I wear extensions almost every day after a butchered haircut took 12 inches of my hair. Authentic human hair extensions come in wefts of varying widths, usually one clip wide or four clips wide, and the hair is very thin because the wefts are meant to be layered on in sections, like natural hair. This blue hair extension is 5 clips wide--wide enough to fasten on your head from ear to ear. Because of my experiences with authentic human hair extensions, I was expecting this blue piece to be thin and underwhelming, but I was wrong and am very happy about that.

I also have an asymmetrical haircut that is close-cropped on one side, so this extension is actually too wide for me to wear across my nape (as the model does in the product image), but I am still able to use it. I curve it to clip from my left nape to above my right ear and it suits my needs and still looks real, despite its unusual color.

Since there is so much hair on a single weft, as other reviewers have stated, this hair extension is a bit heavy. That may take some getting used to, as improper clipping could cause some discomfort and irritation from pulling on the roots. However, once you get practice putting it in, you will learn the best way to clip it without any tugging or pulling of the hair.

It is synthetic material, and while I noticed that mine was shiny, it was not like "Barbie hair," as some reviewers said. I did not need to rub any baby powder in; I just wore it to work for a few hours a day and the shine eventually dulled to look a little less like synthetic material. I will try to add some photos to this page of what the extension looks like in my (straight black) hair. I also noticed that it became easily tangled after a few days of wear. This is not really a problem; I am afraid to brush it too often though, as it sheds (which is normal, but I want it to last).

Lastly, despite being synthetic material, I have tried straightening it on the high setting on my Conair flatiron with success. You can also use a razor or scissors to make any modifications to the style or length, if necessary.

Overall, I think you will be very satisfied with this product if you buy it. I liked it so much that I placed an order for dark pink/light pink and yellow/lime green. These were not add-on items, however, so I will be waiting until mid-August to September to receive these other hair extensions. Hopefully they will be as nice as the blue/peacock green, because I was not disappointed!

title: Save time with Semi-Permanent Eyebrows
text: I don't know why I just found out about this now. I've been pencil shading in my eyebrows everyday for 22 years. This product is amazing! It tint last about 1 week before it starts to fade, which is better than shading it in everyday. My eyebrows are what I spend the most time on when I'm doing my makeup so when I saw this, I had to try it for myself. One capsule is a lot of powder so I only use 1/3 of it w/ an equal amount of developer for both eyebrows. The package comes with a eyebrow brush and the application is easy. If you accidentally get the tint on the outside of your brows, just quickly wipe it off with a damp q-tip. I suggest going on YouTube to get some tips on how to apply it before actually putting it on yourself. I definitely recommend this product. It's pretty awesome! :-)

But I would not recommend nor will I purchase this again

title: It's hard to put into words how amazing these feel
text:
I buy a LOT of items from Amazon. Only about 10% of the time do I feel compelled to write a review. I've looked to buy an anti-fatigue mat for a couple years now. I finally bought two of these for our kitchen. It's hard to put into words how amazing these feel. Although it would look odd, I wish our whole downstairs was floored with this stuff. I walk into the kitchen now, and I find myself looking for an excuse to walk on them. "Can someone do the dishes?" ME ME ME!!!

I shopped around and looked at a ton of anti-fatigue mats. I finally settled on these because (1) outstanding reviews, (2) even though they're more expensive than others, I took a chance on the adage that "you get what you pay for" (they come with a 20 year warranty), (3) there is no design imprinted on the top (thus, dirt cannot get trapped in any grooves). I only want to buy such mats once. These were pricey, yes, but I don't want to buy a cheaper one that wears out in a couple years and then have to buy a new set.

All in all, these are outstanding.

title: Wow! Even better than expected!!
text:
I've bought this brand before and really like their bags. In fact, my recent travel experience, including domestic and int'l flights, was made so much another and easier thanks to their travel bags. So when needing more toiletry bags, I bought these hoping for a similar experience. These bags are even better. They have double handles, which is a BIG PLUS, and the zippers are not only super sturdy but also have large circular zipper pulls (two), which is awesome. These details make the difference between an ok bag and one that you actually enjoy using. Strong material all around, including a bottom design that lets the bag stay upright and keeps its shape while also offering flexibility.
Since you get 3, there enough here for different items, storage at home, even everyday use. And here's a surprise bonus which just occurred to me as I opened these: You can even use one as a purse at venues that have strict "clear bag only" security policies, since that's a thing now. Highly recommend!

Along the way I've also read about the dangers chemicals in deodorant and other skin care products. title: Great Toothbrush, Especially for College
text:
I've used this toothbrush, the essence 5600, and the flexcare r910. The flexcare is more powerful and has better brush heads (it also avoids the problem of having water trapped inside the brush and growing mold), but it's also far more expensive. The essence 5600 is a waste of money in my opinion as it has the same performance as this toothbrush, but suffers from a shoddy li-on battery that dies after about a year or so of use. This avoids the battery problem by using replaceable AA batteries and lasts quite a while on each replacement.

The only major con is with the brush head design. There is a a gap between the brush body and head, and this gap tends to fill with water after use. This unfortunately leads to black mold growing in the cavity. It's easy to avoid this by simply unscrewing the head after brushing and rinsing out the cavity, but it's an annoyance nonetheless.

All in all, this is a great cheap way to get the benefits of sonicare.
