title: Dark circles are brighter, the eyes look more awake, and the skin is supple and in prime condition as a base for makeup.
text: This is Jacob's wife writing the review of the Valentia True Glow Eye Cream. My biggest problem spot when applying makeup is the dark circles under my eyes. So, naturally, I am interested in products that claim to solve this problem. The Valentia True Glow Eye cream comes with beautiful packaging, an easy to use nozzle for dispersion, and simple to follow instructions for application. I tried the eye cream both at night before bed to see the overnight results and also in the morning before makeup application. I liked using the cream right before makeup application the best. It works well as an eye cream, but I like the fresh and well rested effect I get when I put it on in the morning. The cream feels a bit tacky to the touch a few moments after application, but this effect quickly fades as the cream soaks into the delicate skin around the eyes. I have very sensitive skin, and there was not a trace of stinging or irritation. The product has a pleasant and subtle smell. I love that this product uses great ingredients. You can tell how high quality the product is when you smooth it onto your eyes. The eye cream gets a 5 star rating from me for all of these reasons, but mostly because it simply works. It isn't magic, and it doesn't claim to be concealer, but it drastically improves the condition of the under eye area. Dark circles are brighter, the eyes look more awake, and the skin is supple and in prime condition as a base for makeup application.

title: These are great stamping plates!
text:
These are great stamping plates. I've owned many plates made by different manufacturers good and bad. These are as good as anything that I have. I've tried stamping a few of the images and they all are very clear and crisp. I did these quickly on paper. The plates seem to be engraved well. It comes with two different kinds of stampers plus two scrapers. Both of the stampers work excellent. I would definitely buy these again and others made by Bluezoo. In fact I will be looking on Amazon for more plates made by them. If you are new to nail stamping one thing to know is that you must take the protective covering off of each plate, it peels right off. It takes some practice but once you have the hang of it you will love nail stamping. I would definitely recommend this set, it would be a good starter set because it comes with scrapers and stampers. I would also recommend using thicker nail polish her polish made specially for nail stamping. It seems to be easier to use than trying to use common nail polish which tends to be a little thin and often smears. I am happy with this product and would highly recommend it.

title: great set of hair clips--flexible hold and durable for styling
text:
I LOVE these!! I have recently gotten into dyeing my hair crazy unicorn colors and I've gotten sick of ruining my really expensive hair clips with all of the permanent dyes. So I decided to invest in this 6-piece set of clips since I've seen my hair stylist use these when she is separating my hair into different pieces.

Anyway, I have really long and thick hair. So when I'm dyeing my hair, which I do at home, I really need to section the hair pieces off to make sure I get all of the strands saturated with color. Otherwise, well, I'm not really into striped hair at the moment and uneven dye jobs that LOOK like I did it myself at home after drinking too much wine ;)

I got the RED color because I have purple hair and wanted the clips to stand out. I do not plan to wear these out of the house--they are only for my styling needs at home. Otherwise, I would recommend getting a darker color because the plastic isn't super nice or stylish looking. I like that these clips open easily and don't have a super strong grip. Some of my clips are way too tight and I end up pulling my hair out or getting my hair stuck in the coils. I like the more flexible feel of these and that they have more give, so I can move them around. Even though they are flexible, they still hold the hair in place, but just do it less harshly, if that makes sense.

I like the set of 6 because I tend to lose track of my clips and so this way, I will have a nice sized supply and won't always be running around my house searching for clips.

I was able to purchase this item at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased review. I hope you found my review helpful and that I provided enough detailed information so that you can determine whether or not this product is right for you and your lifestyle needs. I take my reviews seriously and try to accurately reflect the true value of the item and not the discounted price I was lucky enough to pay. Enjoy!

title: Great for touch-ups
text:
After years of electrolysis, every once in a while, I will get a stray hair that pops up. I don't really feel like driving the hour to the nearest electrologist and buying a whole session for just one hair. For this purpose, this little machine is excellent.
If you've never had electrolysis, I wouldn't recommend it. Knowing what to expect this to feel like has helped me use it effectively.
Looking to remove all the hair on your legs? Unless you want to take this on as a full time job, I wouldn't recommend it. It is very slow.
The included tweezers are next to useless. You probably have some at home that work. Use those.
Since I am not professionally trained, and this is not a professional machine, I find it is easiest to use on thick hairs. I have a very difficult time trying to use the probe on thin hairs.
Like the other reviewers, I find the beep annoying. I'm sure there could have been a better way to design this to indicate when the current is on. But at least I know it's working.
At this price, for touch-ups, it is perfect. Keep your expectations in line with the price.

title: Grew My Thinning Hair
text: This gel actually filled in my hair. My hair had thinned out on the top thanks to menopause and heredity. I absolutely saw results. It took about 6 months of use before I noticed my hair filled in. I used it two to three times a  week to slick down my bun. However, I can no longer use it. Sadly, my face does not like biotin. I didn’t make the connection that the gel was breaking my face out until very recently. I started battling adult acne around the time I started using it and attributed it to hormones. I tried prescription Retin-A, ProActivMd, Differin Gel and many other things over the past year and a half. Nothing cleared it up. Recently, I replaced it with Xtreme Wetline because I wanted more hold. After only a week of not using it, my face completely cleared.  If you’re looking for hair growth, this works! If you’re sensitive to biotin, be mindful of possible breakouts.

title: Not the super-exfoliator I wanted
text: I was not impressed with this foot mask. It claims to be exfoliating, so I expected it to work like other foot exfoliating masks I’ve used in the past. The other foot masks are used like these are - there’s a gel distributed inside some plastic booties. You open the top of the booties, put your feet in the booties, massage the gel through the bootie so your entire foot is covered with gel, tape the bootie closed around your ankle them sit back and relax for an hour or so. The other masks Ive used took about three days for the exfoliation to start - I would shed thin sheets of skin for several days and at the end be left with amazingly soft uncalloused feet. This did not happen with this product. No shedding, no exfoliation. My feet may have been a little softer immediately after washing off the product, but that is the only benefit I received. I thought maybe I didn’t do it right with the first set in this 3-pack, so a month later I got out another pair of booties, read the instructions super carefully, left on the booties for the longest recommenced time and waited to see if I’d get better results this time. Nope. Will go back to ordering one of my previous brands

title: About this book ...
text:
The first edition of The Presence came out in 1945 and went through two printings. Berthold von Schenk had by then been a LCMS parish pastor for 27 years and was serving his third parish, Our Saviour Lutheran Church in the Bronx, New York. The material came from years of sermons, lectures and retreat meditations. The Presence is his confession of what the Holy Eucharist means to him and what it can mean for us: the actual presence and self-giving of the living Christ.

The 65 years since its original publication have not diminished the power of The Presence to illuminate the well-known narratives and doctrines of our faith in striking ways. It would be a rare pastor or layperson who reads it through without recognizing thereafter holy ground not seen before in worship and life, and without an ever-deepening love for Holy Communion.
This edition is an updated printing of von Schenk's original 1945 edition. Footnotes and an introduction are provided by The Rev. Paul Robert Sauer, current pastor of Our Saviour Lutheran Church in the Bronx, New York.

title: Seriously the best wig! People thought it was my real hair!!
text:
This wig is THE BEST QUALITY wig I’ve ever tried that wasn’t real human hair! Everyone was amazed they the quality, & a few people at our costume party asked me if it was my real hair that I just dyed! I wish this company made more wigs because I want more! If you can afford to spend a little more, & want to the wig to look realistic, get this! It is much heavier than other costume wigs which is a plus for me because it’s so realistic & solid quality. It could be a con for some who want a lighter wig.

The only con is the hair layers start coming loose in the braid as you move around, so you’ll have to rebraid it after a couple hours. And it did come kind of curled up & one of the braids is curved while the other is straight on the ends, but I’m sure it’d flatten out if I put some weight on it for a time. And the wig did start sliding off my head, but it’s probably because my hair underneath is thick & long so it’s likely not the wig! It also didn’t flatter my yellowish Asian skin tone, but that’s not the fault of the wig, since Anna is a Caucasian character 😋 All in all, it’s a great buy! ☺️😍🥳🎉❤️❤️❤️

title: Best liner to brighten your eyes and turn shadow into liner - I'm a believer!
text:
I was initially skeptical of a rose-gold liner, and when I first applied it it didn't look that dramatic on my very pale skin. However, I quickly learned how to use it best for my skin tone: to line the rims of my lower lids, which are frequently red and tired. It makes me look more awake and gives my eyes more of a shine than using basic white liner on that area. It's a godsend when it comes to getting ready for work in the morning after a night of insomnia or whatever else you've been up to. If you have darker skin, then this color will look fun on you just as a liner! But I also like to use it to line my upper lids to use as a base for using eyeshadow as a liner. It's basically like primer for a softer liner look, and I apply eyeshadow with a flat brush on top of the rose gold.

It will stay for as long as you need it to - I recently put it to the test when I was unexpectedly called to the hospital when my best friend went into labor! I put on my makeup in the morning, had been at work all day, called to the hospital at night, barely got any sleep, and the baby was delivered at noon the next day...so that was almost 30 hours of wearing the rose gold liner under my eyeshadow liner and on my lower eye-rims, and my eye makeup was STILL in place after that baby arrived! (I also wore Blinc mascara, which I also love for its staying power!) I've also worn it on several cross-country flights (including red-eyes) in order to arrive looking somewhat rested. It definitely works!!

You do have to sharpen it fairly regularly - I'm not a fan of the sharpener that comes on the cap, so I keep a small sharpener next to my makeup mirror. I do, however, LOVE the smudgy tool on the other end - it works great! I should buy this in bulk, that's how much I love it! I went from being a skeptic to incorporating it into my makeup routine every single day.

title: Was first skeptical....
text: Honestly, when I took this out of the box I thought, "geesh...I just wasted 18 dollars," but I gave it a shot on my incredibly long, wavy, crazy hair, and it was worth every penny.  This brush is very light and unassuming.  The bristle design helps tremendously with tangles, something I have to battle multiple times per day.  I usually cringe at the thought of brushing my hair out after a shower, since, it typically is a tedious and painful battle, but I commend the designers of these brushes.  For the first time in my life I didn't have to spend 30 minutes of my day picking through my hair with a comb/brush, in front of a mirror, and ripping out multiple rats.  The design of this brush cuts down on detangling time and did not cause me the pain that I am used to.  Not to mention, it doesn't rip out a handful of hair every time I brush with it either. title: Nice shaver for the price but cleaning it is tough
text:
This is a good product. I haven't used any other to compare it with and
only used it once so far. There is an amazing assortment of attachments.
The primary rotary shaving heads do a good job but if your hair is longer
than a few millimeters, its best to use the clipper attachment first to
trim it down and then use the rotors. The biggest let down is the clean
up process. Hair is tightly compacted into the rotary blade housing.
I used tweezers, a dental pick, and compressed air and still couldn't
get all of the hair out. The mechanism is small and delicate so you have
to be careful. The instructions indicate that water can be used but I haven't
tried that yet. The instructions are brief and awkwardly worded. There are
helpful demonstrations on the internet.
This is a follow up, one month later. When I tried to use the shaver a second
time, it shut itself off with a message that not enough power was available.
Even though the charge display showed 88 to 90% available. I tried cleaning
it again and even used water. Some hair remained jammed in. I am waiting
for a response from the seller.

title: Love it, with a few conditions
text:
Okay, I've got crazy sensitive skin and basically can't use any antiperspirant and many natural deodorants without having a reaction. I've tried at least 20 different brands- Tom's, Herban Cowboy, Lume, Weleda- and I've tried roll-ons, sticks, creams, powders, you name it. I work up a sweat at work every day and unfortunately for me and my laundry basket, I can be a stinky person. This is one of my favorite natural deodorants I've tried, but it does have some drawbacks:

Pros:
1. Smells delightful. It is an earthy, aromatheraputic type scent (not perfumey). If you like Aveda products you'll like how this smells. And an extra pro, the smell is not overwhelming- I can't even smell it when it's on unless I smell my armpit directly. Nice!
2. Good staying power. Unlike most roll-ons I've tried, it dries quickly and at the end of the day I can still smell it if I smell my armpit. I do smell some sweat, too, but it doesn't smell like b.o. Nice!
3. No bad skin reactions after 2 months of use. First product I've used in YEARS that I got away with using this long. Very nice!
4. Washes out of fabrics. Some other essential oil type deo's I've used in the past have left oil marks on the armpits of my shirts. I've had no issues with this product. We love it!
Cons:
1. Pricier than a lot of other brands. I've been using it as recommended, morning and evening, for about 7 weeks, and I have about 1/5 left of the bottle.
2. Like every other roll-on, does nothing to soothe chafing if you're wearing a tank or t shirt or you move around a lot.
3. On hot, busy days, a midday reapplication goes a long way. I don't have time/access to do this at my work, so some days I just get stinky. But I will say, I don't get as stinky as I have with a lot of other products.

Bottom line, I'll buy it again so I don't run out, but I'm still on the hunt for the *just right* one for me. Overall, 8/10. Tics most boxes for me, and the scent is really lovely.

title: Perfect for skin + hair on newborns- great ethnic option!!
text:
This was a great product made to be helpful For moisturizing but, also being sensitive enough for use on a newborns skin. The baby i had ordered this for, has a unique combo- half Icelandic and half African… Creating skin mixture that needs very delicate and natural products that still can provide plenty of moisture. This product delivered!!

After noticing a few dry spots on baby skin, I did some research on how to re-capture moisture necessary.. my concern was a bit of cradle cap and the use of Emelia’s baby bath oil on the scalp. Not only was it very sensitive and user-friendly, it took care of the issue within a few days.. The other concern was the possibility of getting product in babies eyes, if added to the overall bathwater.. of course, I used my best efforts to keep this from happening- but, I found dipping washcloth into the water and wiping off face… Absolutely caused zero problems- I was totally surprised and impressed!!

Knowing the African culture needs a bit of moisturizer in skin care as well as hair care… This has covered both needs in just this one product. I’m certain as this newborn grows we may need to adjust course and add more specific hair care regiment. As for now, this is doing just fine- and best of all leaves baby smelling amazing!!

So, if looking for a product that is gentle enough to use on a newborn… That has taken the precautions to keep their skin safe… Using only natural products- and has the ability to moisturize and even clear up cradle cap.. just a few drops of this to the bathwater- can handle the trouble spots no problem.. loving it!!

title: Most comfortable electric shaver I've used
text:
As someone who's never grown a beard or mustache (I can go maybe a day or two but after that it drives me crazy), this has to be the most comfortable electric shaver I've ever used. The three pivoting heads move over facial contours very easily, and I appreciate that it's quieter than others I've used. In spite of that, it took a week or so to feel like it was doing a good job - perhaps because my face needed time to adjust (as claimed in the manual) or because it takes me a while to figure out how to use it most effectively - but I'm happy with the job it does. The neck has been the hardest area, and I've still got a few rough spots on the jawline, but it's much less than at first. The only real "negative" is the very small trimmer, which is placed rather awkwardly and doesn't seem to work very well.

Another nice plus is the LED display showing exactly how much time is left before needing to be recharged, and each charge seems to last much longer than others I've used (betweeen 2 to 3 weeks, and it only took an hour to fully charge). You can't use it plugged in, but it will allow you to plug it in for a "quick charge." The display also shows when to clean it (about every other use) and the heads flip up for easy cleaning under running water (much easier than using the tiny brush). It looks great, too - very sleek and cool and stylish - with a nice little charging stand, which looks good enough to leave out on the counter (minus the cord). It also comes with a "charging pod" which will be good for travelling.

Overall, I'm very pleased with it, although $240 is pretty steep. It's still not "as close as a blade" but I've given up believing that any electric shaver truly can be, but it's a whole lot more convenient that using shaving cream. (Others have complained about the "excessive" and difficult packaging, and I must agree completely on that point - use a knife carefully or scissors).

title: I’m never going to buy a different brand of mascara.
text:
This product is AMAZING. It never clumps so shortens “getting ready” time significantly. The gliding smoothness takes a bit getting used to. That typical mascara thickness just isn’t there. But it didn’t take me long to get the hang of it and love it. My lashes now have a natural, full, thick look to them. One or two coats is all it takes.

It’s waterproof in a gentle way. Never melts down, but is super easy to remove with my Trader Joe’s organic coconut oil. I even use it on my eyebrows, brush and all. Works great. Same ease of waterproof application.

I had already gotten great lash growth thickness with the NuSkin product, but it has only gotten better with this mascara. I’m so happy I switched from my old waterproof Maybelline fixation. I’m 67 and had previously been considering extensions. Now I can even go out without any makeup and feel good about the way I look. I’m living proof that the high quality organic ingredients are a better fit for our bodies.

It lasts a long time too and doesn’t dry out in the container. I’m starting month four on my first tube and it’s still not empty or drying out.

It feels great to finally find a company offering high quality, organic make up at affordable prices. Gonna see if they have a concealer next for those occasionally dark eyelid areas closest to the nose. I’ll do a review on it too.

title: Works for Eye Puffiness & Amazing Off-Label Headache Relief!!
text:
This eye cream was far different than I was expecting -- but in a GREAT way. I'm 29-years-old, so the lines around my eyes aren't terribly deep, although they are probably deeper than many other females my age due to my sun-worshiping in earlier years. I do suffer, however, from severe seasonal allergies and am constantly plagued with dark circles ("allergic shiners" as they call them) and extreme puffiness of both my upper eyelids and just under my eyes (the infamous "bags").

To be honest, my impression of this cream at first glance wasn't too great. For starters, the jar of cream was much smaller than I had imagined, but I guess that's my fault for not noticing the (very) small-printed "0.5 fl oz" on the label in the picture. (I do think that this was a little deceitful though, because the size/amount of this cream is not listed anywhere on the product description.) In addition, while trying to read the teeny tiny print on the jar, I also almost immediately noticed a spelling typo on the front of the label that reads "Nautral & Healthy" rather than "Natural & Healthy." I know this seems like a rather minuscule and knit-picky detail to point out, but it did make me at least wonder about this company's quality control standards.

Despite my initial impression, I gave the company/cream the benefit of the doubt and have been using it for the last few days. With my first application under my eyes, the cream felt more on the "thin" side (similar to a lotion rather than thick cream), but while rubbing the cream into my skin in a circular motion, the moisture that the cream delivered was far better than I anticipated. It takes at least 3-4 minutes before your skin feels like the cream is completely absorbed, which gave me ample time to slowly massage the area during absorption (and felt very soothing and relaxing). I had absolutely no issues with any side effects or skin reactions such as burning, tingling, redness, or irritation. I noticed a small "immediate" reduction in the pores and fine lines of the areas I applied the cream, though not as impressive as some of the Retinol and Vitamin C serums that I've used in the past. There was no obvious difference in the dark circles or puffiness right after application, however, within about 30-45min, the puffiness started to visibly decrease and the area all around my eyes felt less tender/achy in general.

With each subsequent application, I noticed the same resulting decrease in puffiness as was observed initially. My puffiness did return each morning, but I expected because I'm still constantly being exposed to my allergens. (After all, the cream is meant to relieve a symptom of my allergies, not be a miracle cure for my chronic systemic condition.) I've noticed that if I apply the cream immediately before bed and spend more time rubbing it into my skin in the circular motions, I do have a noticeable decrease in the morning when I wake up compared to before I started using the cream. As far as the under-eye dark circles, I can't tell a difference so far - but in all fairness, I've only been using the cream for 4 days (twice daily) so there's still hope.

Now for the best part (in my opinion, at least). On the second night of using this cream, I had yet another one of my throbbing headaches (allergies are the worst!!) About 15-20min after my evening cream application, I noticed that my headache felt a little better -- I'm not saying that there was some miraculous change here, but it did feel less severe. This was very unusual for me, as I hadn't taken any ibuprofen or Zyrtec at that point (my usual go-to medications when my headaches are severe). The only thing I could think that I had done differently this time than any other time was that I applied the cream -- it was a strange connection, but stranger things have certainly happened to me. I went back to look at the ingredients listed on the jar and saw that it had rooibos in it. I had absolutely no idea what this was (and probably didn't notice it the first time I looked because I don't have a magnifying glass), but I looked it up online and read that it's an herb from South Africa with a laundry-list of health benefits -- including antioxidant properties and... headache relief?!?! Although still relatively skeptical that this was just a coincidence and that I had gotten "lucky" on my google search, I was still optimistic and proceeded to rub the eye cream on my temples and at the base of my skull to see what would happen. After a good 30-45min, my headache was almost completely gone. I know this headache thing sounds ridiculous and obscure, but I encourage anyone to research this mysterious Rooibos for themselves. I haven't had a headache as severe as that one since, but I have had a few headaches start to come on, and with each headache, I applied the cream to my temples, under my eyes, and to the base of my skull and the feeling has gone away each time within about an hour.

With all of this said, I couldn't rate this "eye" cream turned headache remedy anything less than 5 stars despite my initial less-than-thrilled first impression. Because the cream is light and only requires a very small amount with each application, I think the small jar will actually last me a reasonably long time, too. Between the headache relief and the disappearing eye bags, I would be confident in recommending this product to others. I was also fortunate to receive my first jar of this cream at a discounted price or free in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.

text:
this is not the 'model' I received. I received the version with 1 large compartment on the front (as opposed to the pictured 2 compartments). I was packing for a job, and wanted to use right away so I did not contact seller to see about an exchange.

as usual, whoever makes stylist's bags does not seem to test the spaces with actual tools inside, fully loaded - as most stylist would use it. the right side has a heat resistant pocket for hot tools. it fits my flat iron & a standard length curling iron (mine must be long as it sticks out the top). difficult to use with tools still hot as the cords really have no space other than inside this pocket. there are envelope slots in the 'door' of this compartment. I have tried to fit a brush ... but it quickly becomes too bulky and difficult to zipper shut.

I use zucca cases inside the main compartment which work fine. I specifically ordered this because it was soft-sided ... but that means when fully loaded, it collapses easily and I really watch how I set it up. also, as I am using it with zucca bags (as opposed to the boxes shown) - getting into just one bag at a time is difficult since they rely on being stacked just so. my zucca bags do not protrude out of the compartment, but fit snuggly in which, again, becomes a problem when using the pockets & accompanying bags that are in this front 'door'. I usually have to lay the bag back to get it zipped close (like the 80's - laying on the bed to zipper your pants!! C-: )

it seems sturdy enough; the proportions of the backpack aspect are good and I really like the heavy duty top handle. time will tell ... my zucca case started coming apart at the seams after just 1 year. hoping this one is better reinforced

title: Kaeline is great!!!
text:
This past summer my family and I spent two weeks in Morocco. During that time we visited women's cooperatives that were producing argan oil products near the beach town of Essouirra. Of course, I bought some products. Upon our return to the United States, I was disappointed to find that I could not purchase argan oil beauty products domestically. Then, to my great surprise I found that one company--Xandra Renouvelle--had become the sole distributor of Kaeline argan oil products in the US. Since August I have NOT stopped using Kaeline products. The Kaeline argan oil products have reduced the number of perimenopausal breakouts that I've experienced each month. I've used Kaeline argan oil on my occasional cold sores and have found it more effective than the more popular cold sore medications. Kaeline has also improved the texture of my hair and provided desparately needed moisture to my arms and legs.

I support Kaeline's mission in that it derives its argan oil from wholly owned and run women's cooperatives in Morocco. These cooperatives are designed to provide income to women, many of whom are single mothers or widows.

Kaeline is certified by EcoCert, a European agency that supports organic and fair trade products.

Since discovering discovering argan oil, I have remained a faithful customer of Kaeline.

Only things I don't like are, the blue light on the dash mount remote control is irritating as hell. Sharpie took care of it. The two knobs on the remote that control frequency sweep and gain are too close together and tiny. They should have gotten rid of that insanely bright blue light and put lights in the knobs so you can see your settings in the dark. And because this is a bass equalizer, if a d.j. or advertisment come on the radio where people are talking, they sound synthesized and unnatural. title: Moistens
text:
Cucumbers are supposed to be amazing for the skin. So, I wanted to give these a go.

Pro: - moisten. Very moist.
- mask doesn't rip or tear
- plastic backing for easier application
- gentle, pleasant aroma
- sinks quickly into skin after massaging at end

Cons: - thin
- hard to slide into place...and then doesn't fit quite right
- suggested wear time is 20-25 mins....longer than usual

These masks come individually packaged in robust and easy to open pouches. The mask has a sturdy, plastic backing, which is the only reason it can be unfolded and placed on without any trouble because these are thin. Surprisingly, they don't rip or tear easily, though. After taking off the backing, it takes a little maneuvering to get these to sit in place. Firstly, the thin material wanted to bunch and roll along the edges of the face, and the lack of thickness made it difficult to get to lay flat. Also, the eye holes aren't quite right. I, my son, and my daughter tried them (each with very different face sizes) and all of us had trouble with the eyes. When they were in position, The bridge of the nose wouldn't stay pressed against the skin, but kept lifting up. The suggested wear time is 20 to 25 minutes, which is longer than the 15 to 20 I normally see on masks. Removing the mask, however, is no problem, and there's a good amount of moisture left over to massage into the skin....which also works well

The masks are moistening and have a gentle, pleasant odor (although I can't really place it). These are refreshing and leave the skin shiny. If only they'd sit better, I'd enjoy them more.

title: Makes teeth sensitive. Use a good manual toothbrush.
text:
I bought a 2 pack of Braun electric brushes for me and my wife. After using it for a month I returned it. I usually do not return any item, least a personal item. But within 3 weeks I noticed that my teeth were more sensitive to cold water, etc. I did not want to use or pay for something that actually ruined my teeth. Looks like these brushes erode the enamel. Once eroded the enamel does not build back. Though I used these brushed around 1 year back, my teeth are still sensitive ever since I used them. The 2nd brush was for my wife and she had exactly the same experience as me.

So I would advise stick to your traditional brush. More technology is not always good.

title: Doesn't do a thing for me
text:
This is a very thin, clear oil. It isn’t as thick as baby oil and runs almost like water. I would never put oil or an oil-based product on my skin because it clogs the pores. Some people might be able to get away with it, but even though I have dry skin, my skin will break out if I put oil on it, so I have skipped that test. I did rub it into my hands and it dries fast without leaving an oily slick.

What I did use this for is my hair, which is very thick and very dry. After shampooing, I poured a little bit of the oil into my hand and rubbed my hands together. Then I ran my hands through my hair, keeping the oil away from the scalp area. Afterwards, I blew my hair dry then applied more oil. Usually, I apply a gel in the same manner that calms the frizz and leaves my hair with a nice shine to it. However, the oil did not produce the same results. My hair still had flyaway ends with no gloss to it. My newly washed and dried hair always looks better the next morning after the hair product has had a chance to calm the frizzy hair shafts, but the morning after I used the oil, the results were no different than the night before.

I’ll stick to my gel product to end the frizziness of dry hair.

title: Easy and nice looking
text: Ease of application is great.  I bought the light/ medium for face as it’s winter and I look pasty.  My body holds a tan through the winter so…. Anyway.  Do Not Do what I did.  I applied and buffed out with a brush.  Great.  I only waited 4 hours before applying another coat, and I wish I did not.  2 more hours later and it was clear it was going to be too dark.  My fix which worked.  Nighttime wash face routine with overnight alpha hydroxy acid on.  In the am before shower I mixed baking soda, veg oil, and lemon juice to a paste.  Scrubbed all over and let sit for 5 min then went back and added a little watered down dawn dish soap rubbing over the paste.  Got in shower and rinsed.  Worked great.  Would say I might be slightly too light right now but it’s good enough.  So don’t panic!!  Next time I will wait a full day before applying more.  I use retinol and aha so it’s going to be trial and error.

title: Lavender - if it smells and acts like lavender it is Lavender
text:
Before purchase I have read the reviews and a lot of the negative ones. I was hesitant. I just started dabbling into the whole world of essential oils so it is a problem and huge challenge to find the right kind of oils.
With this oil I was and still am somewhat skeptical as the price is very reasonable and because there was that after or base note of something else lingering at first then second and all whiffs. It goes away pretty quick but it did make me double guess. Then, I just decided to use it as any other lavender oil - a drop on the pillows and a couple on the mattress for a restful night. I was out like a light in no time, so it works just like chloroform does I would imagine without a headache the morning after. Perfect!
Then! My Mom in law was in town and her shingles just popped up - hopefully not being the stressor - so wanted to help her ease the pain and offered lavender oil as a natural solution which she happily accepted. We put it along her spine and a couple drops directly on the shingle spot. Waited and I didn't really expect much, but about 30 minutes into the evening she said "you know what!? My shingles doesn't hurt no more". I'm a believer now and so is she.
Nr. 3 - toddler was in a bind with stuffy nose so mixed 1 drop with 5 drops of coconut oil and smeared a tiny amount at the entrance of the nostrils. He was way less stuffy the next morning and managed to sleep through the night peacefully.

This is good enough for me. And while there were other brands I liked as well, I might just reorder this brand.

title: Portable and, once you get the hang of it, easy to use
text:
My hair type is fine but somewhat full and is shoulder length.

Before attempting to use this device I watched numerous YouTube videos of others using this it (or similar ones made by different manufacturer). I know that helped me A LOT in terms of figuring out how to best use this device. Oh and even these YouTubers, who review hair tools all the time, would sometimes get their hair caught in the barrel.

But once I got into a groove I found I could get my whole head curled in 10 and 15 minutes. Yes, by the 15-minute mark the battery was very low but I never fully ran out of power. Apparently this is a problem with this sort of device from other manufacturers as well.

My curls held up fairly well throughout the day but I'm going to try it on a higher heat setting next time to see if they will stay a little more curly even longer.

Will this be my "go-to" curler 24/7? No.

But I do love the fact that it is pretty easy to use, there is NO CHANCE of burning your finger/hand/head/ear/etc., and I especially like the cordless/portable aspect of it. If I'm getting ready with my friends or I'm sharing a hotel room with friends or family it would be extremely useful have a cordless device. Plus sometimes I just want to sit in front of the TV and do my hair and that's not always possible with some hair tools.

title: So far so awesome: Day 1
text:
Notes out of the box;
* Very nice, low profile footprint design.
* 7 inches long, 5 inches at widest part of the oval, 2.5 inches tall.
* Fits up to .75 oz polishes with all shaped bottle necks (bam! take that iZen Pro!)
* Silver plastic, no idea on solvent resistance (yet).
* Uses both AC adapter or batteries for cordless operation.
* 5600 shakes per minute promises the box and spec detail.
* 3 detachable, adjustable rubber securing straps.
* 1 on/off button, with a lighter pressure 'pulse on' feature.

Operation:
* Cinch to strap on.
* Pretty impressive agitation for one so wee.
* Noise level mild especially in comparison to my incessant bottle agitation whining. "Ow! My arms! Blah, blah, blah." I'd be more specific, but I was operating it right on a stainless steel counter to put it through the noise test. It'd be unladylike to compare it to a 'personal massager' out loud wouldn't it? Did I just type that? Oopsy, my bad. Quieter over a mat, silicone, towel or cork tile by miles. I did that too.

Result:
Before had separation, some streaky clearness and clinging pigment near the neck. I gave it my regular human powered roll/tap/roll for the following 'before shaker' application demo. (See posted pics)

Left, regular human agitation. 5 freaky coats and I had to wrangle dual directions and mucho smoothing in application. I tried really hard to give it more care actually because I wanted to be ungenerous to ForPro. But, human agitation was a streaky, bubbled shrink-back, difficult, wrinkling mess at every application and cure. Just like last time I dared to be decadent. I purposefully used a problem child color of an otherwise fabulous gel polish line. Plus, I left the cleansing paper towel wipes in the photos to demonstrate the horrid vs acceptable cure difference. Like eww! That's dangerous. It even wiped off in spots!

Right, ForPro shaken. 3 coats. Application was easy and like normally behaved other vampy, dark, well agitated gel polishes. Say what? I thought I'd see some improvement, but I didn't really expect this great of one. The color is wearable and I like it now.

All things are equal in my test, except I was more careful in application of the human shaken swatch, which has 2 more coats for a subpar, unusable result. I had to, it was having a tantrum in streaky pants before ForPro.

Other notes:
ForPro requires a phillips head screwdriver to open the 4 AA battery housing. Probably because it shakes so much? Still, a nice cordless option to the AC adapter also included. Yay that. *Batteries not included.

And, I think any warranty would be online via ForPro website only? This didn't come with instructions or warranty card in the box. Mostly a no-brainer plug and play.

It's unscathed by alcohol meaning any gel polish mishaps can wipe right off. I blotted the underbelly to remove dust lightly.

Clear rubber feet stay put while leaving no marks. That's awesome. Especially compared to Robarts that jiggle off tables and sound like landscaping machines.

I like ForPro so far. I think it saves product and my shake stress physiology. Prettier result in fewer, easier coats :).

It did a good job on both a chunky glitter and a streak-prone frost in a conventional lacquer brand. I did shake them once to break surface tension first though. I also rotated the bottles and let them shake twice. The beads were rolling free in both and additive distribution was even and good after. Thanks ForPro! Please last an eon now.

title: To date the fastest, most convenient, least messy shave I've had
text:
This shaver really is a treat to use. I have had no difficulty with the "grade" of shave slipping mid shave and I find it a very comfortable shaver to use.
The shave is quick and the receptacle really does seem to catch upwards of 95% of what I'm using.
So far I've had four shaves off the one charge and I shave once a week, so the battery seems to hold its charge really well.
Cleaning is a cinch.
It's noisy with turbo on, but certainly not in the same vicinity as a hair dryer. It's certainly not anything that's a detraction from the shave.
The only reason I haven't given this 5 stars is because of the area just under the nose, the other reviewers that have mentioned this are correct - it's very hard to get to this area without contorting your nose and/or lip.
I simply remove the protector and do the last few hairs freehand with the open shaver or a pair of cuticle scissors.
The overall performance of this shaver more than makes up for this one shortcoming.
Very nice carrying bag too included.

title: My Favorite Beauty Helper
text:
OMG !!!!!!! I have been cruising Amazon for this exact item for quite some time and was thrilled to see it for an excellent price. I had been to retail and beauty supply stores everywhere I went, only to be told I could not order just the unit. I had already purchased extra rollers with my original unit. My Caruso Steam Unit gave up the after some major miles, through many hair styles.
I have very thick, straight American Indian hair that was prematurely gray hair before I could graduate high school. I purchased the Caruso Steam set and many extra curlers years ago when it first came on the market. I had tried various kinds of steam roller sets that were difficult to wrap my straight hair around. They were pokey and heavy with painful steel pins to hold rollers in my hair; only for the curls to fall out almost immediately. I tried ceramic, adjustable heat settings, and many different curling irons and dryers that was new on the market. I combined hair gels or mousse with them only to have curls and waves fall out within minutes. I went to beauty school (lol) after high school. Really not my thing
The new unit I received is not round like this picture. It is kidney shaped.
The old version I had to add a small amount of salt to the water.
The newer version has a slider switch for OFF, STEAM and IONIC STEAM.
I love, love, love the Caruso Steam for my hair.
I used 6-8 curlers for my whole head.
Literally seconds to put each in and about the time I get them all in it is time to take them out. Just like my old Caruso Steam unit (without the salt).
My curls last three days minimum.
I DO NOT use hair products on my hair ever.
Curls are soft and natural feeling.
Caruso Steamer does not damage hair. Stopped perms many years ago after finding this steamer. This amazing steamer adds shine to my curls. I can run my fingers through my hair as much as I want.
There is never those bent ends of hair, curls just fall, I shake my head, move a curl or two with my fingers and I am out the door.
I have missed this little time saver and am continually complimented on my hair and how much younger I look.
I hope this helps someone, lets me know how you use your Caruso Steamer

title: Terrific facial color
text:
I really like this bronzer, and it's hard to find in stores.
I've been using it since I was 40 (I used to use the clear red, but swapped over to the bronzer when I turned 50).
For an older woman it's much nicer than a heavy foundation and fake-red-clown-cheeks. It provides the correct amount of color and allows us to keep our face out of the sun. For a younger woman you'll get that "oh, you look so beautiful with no makeup" comment all the time. It's a lovely translucence.

As a result, at 60 I have no wrinkles, but maintain a healthy glow. (Of course I get tons of exercise and drink lots of water also)

title: Love this shaver!
text:
I'm a person who has used several types of clippers in the past, both corded and cordless and I've gotta say this is one of the best! Originally discovered it on Amazon when I was in the market to replace my former cordless clipper and was impressed by the high rating. Now I'm singing its praises too.

I've been using it for over a year now and would definitely buy another if I had to... but I don't! It's still going strong with a powerful battery and the suction of loose hair even allows me to shave in the car when I'm pressed for time. I would say as long as you shave in the proper direction (where the blade cuts first and the suction thingy follows) the shaver will suck in at least 85% of the loose hair it cuts. Usually I have very little hair left on the sink after I shave. The fact that it's cordless is a plus and the device gives just enough options with the adjustable guard and the precision blade. A full charge last me long enough for me to forget when was the last time I charged it and I love the fact that it's a good size and weight for travel. A great buy at a good price!

title: Love the Coconut Baby & will try others...
text: ***  Just received the Limited Edition "Sugar Cookie" in the mail today.  My 21-month-old daughter is already asking for a bath while yelling "yummy" -- yes, it smells great! *****  I first tried Sweet Baby Organics Shampoo & Wash in Coconut Baby for my daughter last fall when she was 10 months old.  I liked the scent right away, but I was also impressed with the fact that it rinsed so clean.  I have tried other natural and organic baby washes with a coconut base, and they seemed to leave a "film" on my baby -- I liked that this formula was rich, and thick, without being sticky.  It's true that the foaming is minimal when comparing Sweet Baby Organics to washes with chemical lathering agents (such as SLS) but I found the lather from the coconut base to be "just right" without being bubbly.  We live in Kenya, so when I buy on Amazon I have to wait for my family to send me a box in the mail, or for someone to come over to visit (last fall I brought a supply of it back in my luggage).  My next package arrives in April, and this time we are trying the Vanilla Baby scent. The shampoo is NOT tear-free, but again, that is because of the lack of numbing chemicals in it's formulation -- I simply hold one hand over my daughter's forehead, and rinse her hair with a cup of clean water in my other hand.  One other fact to note is that a little goes a long way.  At first I was concerned that there were only 8oz bottles available, but even at 15 months old, a dime-sized amount cleans my daughter's entire head and body (though I have to admit that being a blondie, she doesn't have a whole lot of hair yet).

title: As advertised - great little travel set!
text: After reading other reviews I purchased this for travel mainly because I liked the extra seal on the jars. I was not disappointed! The jars held my lotions and creams without any spillage. I was equally pleased with the squeeze bottles using them for shampoo, conditioner, body wash and my liquid make up remover. The large openings made it very easy to fill. The lids sealed properly, and dispenced my products in a controlled manner. Added bonus was the built in labeling system, with just a click/turn I was able to choose to proper notation for the product inside (as mentioned in other reviews it is easier to do this when the screw cap is off prior to filling). The sizes are adequate - I had more than plenty product for a week long journey. Overall I am very pleased with this set and will recommend it to friends. The best part is I now have a pre-packed "to go" travel kit ready for the next adventure.

title: Lightly moisturizing; has a wonderful rose fragrance; left my face silky
text:
Surprisingly, I really like this mask. I already tried their [[ASIN:B07VQY9ZP2 WeeklyPoint Cosmetic Saffron mask sheet]] and found it to be very messy and just okay. This one is less messy.

There are ten individually wrapped masks in this package. The instructions are in broken English but still easy to understand. I applied this mask to my clean face -- there are holes for eyes, nose, and mouth. It was somewhat wet but not dripping wet as their other mask. I prefer less solution, so this was perfect. I let it stay on for exactly 20 minutes and then removed the mask and wiped off the excess. My face felt moisturized and silky. I reveled in the soft rose fragrance the entire time.  However, the claims that it brightens the skin and protects them from wrinkles is unfounded in my opinion. On the other hand, the rose fragrance is calming and wonderful and the moisturizing effect is silky and these are my two favorite traits of this moisturizing sheet.

title: A good human hair wig!
text:
613-20”-bangs

I would buy this again! It came in a bag, tangle free and had a slight smell to it. Bangs are long enough to be cut to desired length. In both pictures they are uncut.

The first picture is it straight out of the bag, only styling the bangs with a flat iron. The second picture is after a color bath and styling.

THE ENDS TAKE COLOR QUICKLY! I dipped mine in a purple shampoo bath, leaving the ends out (only allowing the water to touch it when squeezing the water out) and they still took more color than the top of the wig, along with the bangs. Holds styling well, went all day without losing curls even in the snow.

I would say it’s all human hair, I don’t see any plastic hairs or had any melting when styling.

The wig is 20” from top to bottom, though I was expecting the hair to be 20”. It’s a comfortable stretchy mesh cap, and fits my larger head snuggly. My head is about 23” around, so heads 22”-24” should be fine wearing this.

Original review:
While I love the concept behind this bag, and the purpose and use for it was exactly what I was looking for, the material it's made out of is perhaps a bit too stiff for the application. When the bag arrived, it was already damaged, with a small hole at a crease that can't be helped, due to the shape and closure of the bag.
Because I needed this for a pending vacation, and it was the only one like it I could find on Amazon, I went with it. The bag served it's purpose, but because of the damage already present, and the wear and tear that invariably happens during travel, it will likely need to be replaced within the year.

Great concept. Needs some revisions.

title: Inexpensive, works great, but completely died in 10 months (2 Units)
text: I purchased two of these together on a deep discount (half price or something). Kept one at home and one in my car for backup, shaving during commuting or at work. I mixed use between the two and a bic safety razor pretty regularly. The two electric shavers both stopped working within a few days of each other during month 10. They appeared to be fully charged but the motor became very slow and then just didn't start up again. A few times I heard a quick buzz sounding like the motor was trying but couldn't start. I think these are probably brushed motors and the brushes just failed. I took one apart and the battery was fine (just a normal Lithium cell). In the end, it's not that bad of a value - $10 a pop and lasts almost a year. Costs less than the replacement blade head on most name brand electric shavers and less than 3 or 4 disposable safety razors blade sets from the name brands. But I hate filling the landfill, so that's a bummer.

title: Best Blemish Patches
text: Have tried multiple different brands of these hydrocolloid blemish patches and find these to be, hands down, the best.  These patches are a little thinner overall and feature an even thinner edge so they both stay in place better with no lifting and sit almost entirely flush with the skin.  They prevent picking and are themselves difficult to pick at.  They also draw exudate exceptionally well and at least 90% of my blemishes are gone within 8 to 12 hours with zero scabbing and no redness or scarring.  I find that they are easiest applied with tweezers and removed after applying a warm, moist washcloth to them.  They are on the smaller side but I've yet to have a blemish they haven't handled admirably.  And, since my skin is overly sensitive to salicylic acid, I greatly appreciate that these are untreated patches; the straight-up hydrocolloid works just fine.

title: Great for curly hair
text:
I've been using Eufora products for maybe 10 years or more now? I love all of the products I have used. I have naturally curly hair which can be prone to being frizzy or dry, and these products really help. Some I use regularly and others on occasion. The scent on this stuff is amazing.

In fact one of the reasons I got so hooked on trying this stuff was because my hair dresser uses them along with many other products and I was always going around smelling how good she smelled and wondering what perfum she was wearing. However this is the type of scent that doesn't interfere with regular perfumes.

It reminds me of just a light natural scent you might get if you walked by a flower stand. Not overwhelming or sweet or musky but just light and fresh. I get a lot of comments on my hair smelling nice as well.

I use these products:

1. Moisture solution conditioner - regular conditioner
2. Moisture cleanse shampoo - regular shampoo
3. Hydration leave in conditioner - leave-in conditioner
4. Urgent Repair - on occasion as special treatment
5. Beautifying serum - to tame frizz and dead ends daily
6. Sculpture Styling Glaze - when I need some hold on occasion
7. Illuminate Shine Mist - to finish off a look with a light gloss
8. Fortifi Strength Spray - daily to enhance strength of hair
9. Pure Polish Finishing Drops - Works the same as the beautifying serum, except I was say for situations when you have especially course, curly or dry hair. Beautifying serum is a little lighter.
10. Curl 'N Defining Solution - when I need some hold on occasion
11. Curl Revitalizer and Spray - when I don't have time to wash my hair and just want to plump up and freshen the scent of my curls
12. Smooth 'N Straightening Balm - on the occasions when I want to straighten my hair
13. Fixation Styling Wax - Tame the baby hairs around the face, the little fly aways with this

title: Works as advertised and better
text:
I have been using a Norelco T370 trimmer for several months. I bought it for the mini-shaver initially, decided to grow a beard, then trimmed the beard with the beard trimmer attachment. It did a great job of shaping and trimming, but left a mess of clippings in the sink. After reading a million reviews, I decided to try the T980 for it's vacuum feature.

The T980 is great! It does a perfect job of trimming my beard evenly (setting at 7, ultimate beard length to be a 9). The best part is that about 90% of the clippings go into the vacuum receptical with only about 10% being lost. I have not come across any of the negatives (adjustment slips, tugs hairs and on and on) mentioned in other reviews. Unlike my T370, this trimmer lets you know when it's fully charged or about to go dead, and even if it does die in the middle of a session from a dead battery, plugging it in finishes the job. Most other trimmers only work when charged, not on house current.

I can't think of anything this trimmer could do better. In my estimation it's looks, design and operation are flawless. The clipper works well on my beard's 'neckline' and the mini-trimmer keeps the bottom of my mustache trimmed perfectly. I really think the T370 does as good a job trimming as this one does, but will not run with a dead battery when plugged in, and does make a mess with beard clippings all over the place. For those two reasons alone, this trimmer is a BEST BUY!

I am not putting the T370's abilities down in any way. For the price it can't be beat. It trims well and the mini-shaver is still the best way to shave the short hairs on my cheeks above my beard on a daily basis. I use it daily along with the T980 and will continue to. They both have their place. If you don't mind the beard trimmings everywhere, you will be happy with the T370, however if you are serious about perfect beard grooming (and I DO mean PERFECT), with none of the mess, both tools are a must.

The instructions say to warm it against your skin for a few seconds before rolling it on to soften it a bit. They also suggest that you wait a few minutes after applying it before getting dressed so it can absorb into your skin and not rub off onto your clothing.

It does a good job of absorbing your sweat smells so it starts with no scent and you stay with no scent all day. I like that it is a deodorant and not a full anti-perspirant as it still allows you to sweat but keeps you from smelling.

This is a very great product! I do have one purple blood vein that runs down from my right eye that has recently popped up and this cream seems to be helping to make that less visible to me!! title: Works like a Charm!
text:
Excellent for the shower if you have disabilities whether it with your knees or your back. Just put this on the floor of your shower or bathtub. Press the suction cups onto the floor with your feet to keep it from sliding.

Wet the Soapy soles with water, then liquid soap. Place one of your feet on top of the Soapy Soles and rub your foot back and forth on the Soapy Soles to work up a good lather. This automatically works the Soapy lather inbetween your toes. When you’re done with the bottoms of your feet you can pick up the Soapy Soles an, flip it over and now rub the tops of your feet for a complete cleaning.

It’s a God send.

title: Very good
text: I have something called Horner's Syndrome, it's some kind of crazy bizarre nerve damage in which part of my face is really dry and doesn't perspire and other areas perspire way too much. Crazy. It also makes one pupil appear small and the other is noticeably larger, so creepy, but the product can't do anything to help me with that. I decided to try this Mehron Skin Prep Pro, to see how well it could help me with the areas that perspire too much. I poured out a very small amount onto a stainless steel makeup palette that I have and decided to use an eye shadow brush to apply it, I only apply the Skin Prep only to parts of my face that have always had the problem. Generally, I start to apply my makeup and need to stop 3-4 times during process to gently blot the same 4 overly sweaty sections, lightly dust powder, sit with a fan, think breezy thoughts, etc. I was careful not to paint the Skin Prep on the alternate, dry areas, those areas do not perspire and can develop a parched, peely appearance. I waited a few seconds for the Skin Prep to dry on my face before continuing with my makeup. I've only used it 4 times so far, but, touch wood, it seems to be working. My makeup has had a more even application and appearance, even before I was done putting makeup on, I feel like my tone was more even and looked better. When it comes to the mirror, we notice things in so much greater detail than anyone else ever would, I realize no one else may ever notice the difference I notice. It certainly has helped a bit with my bizarre harlequin sweaty vs dry issue I've got going, even if no one else ever notices it, that little improvement means something to me.

title: My one and only
text:
Bottom line: this is the only cleanser I will use.

I got into Mary Kay products because my sister in law was selling it. I thought it was pretty over priced at first but I purchased it anyways since I wanted my sister in law to make her commission. I used it the same night I purchased it.

As soon as I opened the cleanser bottle I smelled a wonderful aroma. It's hard for me to explain it. It was like a very light, sweet fragrance. I love the smell so much! As I was rubbing it on my face, all I could do was keep sniffing and sniffing. I know! I'm such a freak!

The cleanser felt awesome on my skin too. I could feel the little exfoliating beads however it wasn't too much and it wasn't too little. Mary Kay seemed to get it just right. A little bit of the product went a long way too. I only need about two or three small squirts of the bottle to cover my face.

The moisturizer I wasn't as impressed with. I thought the smell was not as nice as the cleanser's. But the moisturizer felt pretty good. It was very light. It felt like my skin could breathe. However, I felt like it wasn't enough of a moisturizer, but then again, I have REALLY dry skin especially during the winter. So I used a small dab of my Elizabeth Arden cream in addition to this moisturizer, so problem fixed. An added bonus is that using the velocity moisturizer I only need a little bit of my Elizabeth Arden thereby extending the life of that expensive cream!

Fast forward a few years, when I was in college. I was a BROKE college student. I couldn't really afford this product anymore. So for about 3-4 years I was using the cheap over the counter cleanser (only $3-4 a pop!). It worked. My skin stayed clear althoughout college but I found myself missing Mary Kay! My skin feel cleaner, more supple and softer while on Mary Kay.

Now that I am a graduate and am working, I am finally able to afford it. I purchased this almost immediately. From now on, I will not skimp out and will order this every time. Actually, now that I am as approaching my mid 20's (eek!) I'll give the Time Wise stuff a go.

I highly recommend this cleanser and moisturizer. The cleanser does its job and does it well. The smell of the cleanser is delicious; I cannot get enough of it. Even though I have to supplement the moisturizer, that's alright. I understand that my skin is probably drier than most normal people. Although this product is a little pricey and not easily accessible, it is so worthit!

title: Cue the squeals of delight: rose-shaped cleansing foam is basic, but big on the cute factor
text:
OK first of all, I did not pay an outrageous price for this. Only $16 usd at a Hong Kong drugstore chain called Mannings. This foam is pure visual pleasure -- shake the pressurized can, and press down on the sides to expel a rose-shaped dollop of cleansing foam. Cue the squeals of delight and instagram envy from beauty junkies.

The foam itself does not remove makeup -- you'll still have to use a cleansing oil first. It's more of a 2nd step cleanser in your dual cleansing routine. If it were not in the shape of a rose, i highly doubt re-buying this --the performance is quite ordinary/basic. But the ROSE! adorable.

For me, the visual gimmick wore off after a while, and I found myself using a normal cleansing foam in a tube, as it was faster and easier than using two hands to pump out the rose each time. Fun to experience one time, but not a re-buy for me.

title: Smells Unpleasant; Barely Notice the Vanilla
text:
I bought the "Vanilla Rainbow Sprinkles" fragrance on another website, as a substitute for Bath & Body Works' "Confetti Cake Pop", which had been sold out everywhere that I had looked online. I was under the impression that the two scents would be similar enough so as to not make much of a difference. I was, unfortunately, very wrong.

"Confetti Cake Pop" is awesome, and it smells just like what the packaging says, "creamy cake frosting." Delicious! "Vanilla Rainbow Sprinkles", on the other hand, is one of the few Bath & Body Works' scents that I heartily dislike. For one thing, the vanilla scent is very muted; not in a good way, as in subtle, but in a bad way, like "I can barely notice the vanilla in this perfume." If that was the only thing about "Vanilla Rainbow Sprinkles" that was off, I could live with it.

After I had sprayed it on myself, however, I became aware that there was a strong under-note of alcohol. I do not wish to smell like rubbing alcohol when I put on scent. Just awful. That bottle will be going into the trash can, and I will not buy "Vanilla Rainbow Sprinkles" in the "mist" (or in any other B&BW product) ever again. It is disappointing, as I usually have good luck with Bath & Body Works, and I enjoy many of their fragrances. I have to also comment that, unusually for Bath & Body Works, the artwork on the product label is very dull and is rather uninspiring.

Looking for a sweet, fun scent that is good enough to eat? Instead of "Vanilla Rainbow Sprinkles", I would recommend Bath & Body Works' "Sugared Snickerdoodle", if you cannot find "Confetti Cake Pop".

title: Been using for years
text:
I bought my first box years ago (when I seem to remember it being the old packaging being labeled as brow safe?). I just finished up another box (3rd? 4th?) and noticed that it’s not meant to be used on the brows. It’s not difficult to keep far away from your eyes, just exercise caution.

I used to apply it for the recommended couple of minutes and got decent results, but now that I’m used to the performance I’ve left it on for as long as 40 minutes and have had the same color payoff but much longer lasting color. I have clear blonde brows despite being a natural dark brunette, so anything I can do to prevent fading helps.

Recommendations:
- Block/outline your brows with a white eyeliner before applying so you can just fill in the shape without having to be too precise with the actual dye- this also keeps skin staining within the area of the brow and less noticeable
- Dye at night or when you can stay in for a couple of days if you’re worried about skin staining
- Dont exfoliate the day of dyeing and the skin stain will come up easier
- use a cotton bud lightly dipped in skin salve/Vaseline to scrub up skin stain rather than washing it away to avoid early fading on brow hairs- even just applying a bit to brows and leaving it on for a while before wiping it away can really get it off much easier AND avoid irritating the skin
- wipe away areas you want to be lighter half way through your dyeing process (Ex: if you want the inner part of the brow to be lighter than the tail, wipe away some dye on the inner part and leave more to develop on the tail)
- You can reapply dye if you feel it didn’t tint enough in one session. Just leave any you didn’t use up on the counter and only discard extra after you’re satisfied with the color payoff (don’t save dye for another day, just go ahead and reapply right after taking off the first application if you feel you want more color)

title: Easy to mix, works as advertised.
text:
Good variety of scents and benefits. Would make great gifts or stocking stuffers. I do like that some of the same benefits can be found in multiple masks so you can reap the benefits over time instead of just randomly. It would be nice to have it by skin type individually too though.

I like the concept of powder since it doesn’t dry out and it’ll keep longer. It states to use 30 ml of water which if you don’t have anything to measure that, the standard medicine cups are usually marked for that if you have those in your cabinet. I did have to use a tiny bit more as it became a paste initially.

There is a lot of product in each tube. Honestly one could probably get away with using half at a time as the whole tube was enough for me to do down my neck as well and I put it on VERY thick. It does dry down though. If it’s too thin it is harder to peel. My skin felt plumped and moisturized. The scent is a bit artificial but more like a pleasant candle than something sickly sweet. Definitely think it’s a good value for the price.

title: Eyebrow game changer
text:
Uh, hello, this product is dope AF. I have suffered my whole life from pretty sparse and invisible colored brows. It's weird, because my hair is pretty dark brown, but my brows look like fishing line and I look like an alien with no makeup on them. I was snooping around the internet doing some research on microblading, which is basically like tattooing on your eyebrows, but it's pretty expensive, and only lasts a few years, and also I don't really trust other people to semi-permanently choose the shape of my brows. I stumbled across this product on Instagram I think and decided to give it a shot.

If you regularly draw on your eyebrows, then applying this will be a breeze. If you have dark hair and wear your brows dark, then you don't really even have to worry too much about how long you keep this product on. I usually do 3 applications at about 7 minutes at a time each time I dye my brows.

I noticed in other reviews that there was some discrepancy on how long this product lasts, and I think I figured out where the difference is coming from. If you have pretty full and nicely shaped brows, but they are light colored and you want them darker, and you are only using this product to dye the ACTUAL HAIRS of your eyebrows, a single application of this will last for weeks. HOWEVER, if you have thin, sparsely furred brows and are using this product to dye the SKIN under and around your actual brow hairs (like drawing or tattooing would do) then this will really only last between 2 and 4 days depending on what you're putting on your face and what kind of activities (like sweating) you are doing.

Either way it is still worth it. It is so nice to wake up in the morning with eyebrows instead of looking like Bob Geldoff in The Wall. I also get about 3 applications from each little "pill" so this product should last a good long while before I have to reorder. Overall very good purchase that I would recommend to anyone who wants darker, fuller brows.

title: Sets make-up on my extremely sensitive skin without irritation
text:
I purchased this product because I needed something to help me set my foundation without the use of powder. I have sensitive dry skin, large pores, and some mild rosecea (sp.?). I use a sunscreen to protect me from getting sunburned and a liquid foundation to cover up the ruddiness. However, when I tried to use powder to set my make-up, it would settle into my pores and make me look ashy and "drag-queeny."

The product does what it is supposed to do. Just make sure you spray it 12" to 16" away from your face. Any closer and you wind up with sticky spots. Also use only 1 to 3 sprays to set your make-up. Anything more than that and again, you'll wind up with a sticky feeling. I mist it on my face after I put on my eyeliner and mascara. It helps prevent me from getting raccoon eyes.

Overall, I like this product and would recommend it if you are looking for an alternative to powder setting your make-up.

title: GREAT toothbrush!
text:
This is a great toothbrush! I have gingivitis and after only ONE use I could see improvement. In addition, it took off every bit of plaque that I could see, i.e. I thought my front bottom gums were really clean and when toothbrush was done, I saw that they weren't as clean as could they could have been.

I hate to floss ("I don't have time right now!") and this does it for me.

In regards to the timer, I had no idea how long two minutes was! It sounds like nothing, and yet I waited and waited for the timer - I thought it was broken but it wasn't; it showed me how me I DON'T take to brush my teeth. Great feature.

The one thing that is kind of hard to get used to is that you just run the toothbrush over your teeth/gums/tongue and I (quite naturally) am used to the back and forth way of brushing and I kept finding myself having to stop working and let the toothbrush do its work.

To sum up, for anyone wanting to get their teeth REALLY clean and not have to put a lot of maintenance into it, you should really think about investing in this toothbrush. It's a really great toothbrush! I KNOW my dentist is going to be happy.

title: I feel like I have brought the nail salon home with me!
text:
I frequently do at home manicures with both gel polish as well as dip powders. It is all fun and games until I need to remove the manicure and dread filing them down/soaking it off. This nail drill has come to my rescue! So far I have only used the sanding wheels but imagine as I get more comfortable with it I will utilize some of the other functions.

I really like that it is rechargeable because nothing is worse than needing to use something and not having batteries on hand. It charged quickly and seems to have hold battery power pretty well as far as I can tell.

The filing/sanding aspect works very well. The design of the machine makes it very comfortable to hold and easy to get a good grip that makes you feel like you have good control over it. It also came with SO many replacement heads for the filing wheel. I now look forward to changing my nail color instead of dreading taking the previous polish off!

title: The good, the bad and the ugly...
text:
My review consists of the good, the bad and the ugly. First,
the good - I finally found a curler that 'fits' my eyelid. Huh, you say? Well, it appears that most of the manufacturers believe I have golf balls instead of human eyes. The tool's shape is long and shallow enough of a curve to catch most of the eyelashes. It is comfortable to use, but takes a bit of practice. Once you are comfortable with the scissor motion (sorry, no other way to describe it) used to make the curl, you are rewarded with a nice, non-crimpy curl. The pad in this one is the best I have used. This tool also opens wide enough that even the most nearsighted or lush lashed amoungst us can get it close to the base of the upper lashes. The design over all is very well thought out. I recommend using an magnifying mirror if you have to use bifocals (I do). All that said, now...
the bad - there is no replacement pad and I am questioning how sanitary this is going to be in the long run. I agree completely with folks here who advise to REMOVE ALL EYE MAKEUP before use. Ain't nothing pretty about an eye infection. I am wondering out loud how to properly clean this curler to be certain it remains safe. If you are prone to sties and other types of infection, I would be asking the same questions. Which leads to...
the ugly - until you practice and are used to it (won't take long) the results are a bit odd looking. The black pad is plushier and looks to be made of silicone and not the usual hard rubber. I have found that the hard rubber pads are chiefly responsible for that weird ugly crimp you can get. Not so with this one. I mentioned before the squeezy scissor action your thumb and index finger have to use can be tricky. If you have coordination issues, well - things could get ugly.
SUGGESTIONS for use...wash your hands before use. I keep the little plastic clip to keep the tool closed. You could follow the advise of one person here to heat the tool with a dryer, but I think I may skip that. Use a makeup mirror to assist you. Practice when you have some time. Enjoy, Ladies - get out there and be lovely.

title: Amazing product and even better customer service!
text:
When I received my shampoo it wasn't packaged correctly so over 1/2 of the bottle had spilled out. I contacted Pure Body Naturals about exchanging for an undamaged product they responded immediately with a coupon code for a brand new bottle. This was super nice of them since there was still about 1/2 of the bottle of shampoo that was usable but they told me to just keep that and sent me a new bottle for free. Amazing hassle free customer service!

And the actual shampoo is AMAZING! I could tell a difference immediately when I used this shampoo. I color my hair and use heat styling almost daily so my hair is damaged and frizzy. After one use my hair is softer and noticeably less frizzy even after blowing drying and styling. Also, since I only wash my hair a few times per week and this shampoo cleans beautifully without stripping my hair. I highly recommend this shampoo!

title: Exactly what I needed
text:
Just the thing I needed to keep my hair tools out of reach of my little ones! Easy to install in 5 minutes, sturdy, functional. I love how I can use it to hold the hot tools while they heat up/cool off so I know that I can walk away (briefly) and be assured my baby won’t pull on the cord. Most other hair tool racks are not made to hold heated tools, just for storage when cool.
I can see how some people might find the slots too small for their curling irons or straighteners, so if you use larger tools I’d recommend something else (for example, the 1-inch tube does not accommodate a 1-inch straightener because it is precisely 1 inch) but this works perfectly for my uses. title: Perfect trio of products for clean and healthy skin.
text:
This combo is heaven! The foaming face wash is light and gentle, and is equally effective in both summer and winter months. I have sensitive skin that develops dry patches, itchy rashes, (or both at once) if I use a product with remotely harsh ingredients. The foam cleanser spreads generously, so it lasts for quite a long time, and it rinses off very easily, reducing the need for unnecessary scrubbing and rubbing. For the last 10 years since I discovered this line, Caudalie has never caused such reactions. The scent is also quite pleasant.

The serum really helps restore elasticity and mousture with ZERO oily feel on the skin. It is one of the very few products labeled "serum" that I find perfectly suitable to use morning and night, before another moisturizer (or without), and under primer and makeup. Again, it's perfect for sensitive skin. It's not lying about helping replenish moisture. It's especially great in the colder months to counter the dry air and constant temperature changes.

The night cream is absolutely fantastic, even without the serum (but using them together is basically a guarantee that you'll wake up with healthy- looking and healthy- feeling, clean skin). It spreads well, so a little goes a long way, absorbs quickly but isn't "disappeared" to the point your face gets dry in the night, and is not cakey or oily (you won't leave a lotion stain on your pillowcase). I HIGHLY recommend this trio. The price is fair and matches what you'd pay at the Caudalie.US store or Sephora.
