title: Soft, silky and light!
text: The best conditioner I've ever used! I bought the shampoo/conditioner set and I haven't used the shampoo long enough to see if it also deserves 5 starts, but the conditioner earned 5 stars after the first use! O wash my hair about every other day and on the 2nd day my roots are usually greasy and my ends are dry and brittle. I've used lots for conditioners that make my hair soft on the first day, but this one kept my ends soft and silky days after I washed it. My hair feels a whole lot healthier! I use it in the shower as a normal conditioner and then I also rub a dab through my ends as a leave-in conditioner. It never makes my hair greasy or heavy. It just feels soft, silky and light. It is definitely costly and that's a big down side, but I didn't take away any stars for that because it did exactly what it says it does for my hair and I bought it knowing the price ahead of time. My favorite conditioner that I've ever tried so far!

title: Just like the more expensive rechargeable version
text:
When I first got this I thought it was going to be a weak version of its rechargeable cousin but boy I was wrong! Performs just like the more expensive rechargeable and without needed a charging base sitting out on the counter. I bought this wanting something I could travel with. The only thing missing in my opinion is a travel case. I also have the rechargeable version although I haven't checked to see if the case that came with that will work with this unit.

The replacement brush heads for the rechargeable unit work interchangeably with this one so that's a bonus. I did take note of what other reviewers said about the battery compartment allowing water to leak in so each time I change batteries I take a cheap lip balm and rub it over the rubber o-ring for a better seal.

In my experience a fresh set of alkalines last well over two months with daily use. I haven't tried rechargeable batteries but suspect they won't last as long due to the lower voltage.

My stylist upon initial installation, remarked this was “bad” hair. This was marketed as human, but this is a human blend at best; good synthetic at worst. The dye line is severe-there is an inch of black and a ton of the gold; it doesn’t blend or gradually merge into the gold. For one thing that makes my skin feel greasy but for another I often forget to add the sunscreen so having them combined into one product is very convenient. Even though a lot of sunscreens tend to make my skin feel greasy that is not the case at all with this moisture whip. I also like that it contains natural SPF protection, I have heard bad things about the chemicals in many types of sunscreen. Disclaimer: I received at a discount in exchange for honest review. title: I highly advise that you stay away from this kit if you want to keep the hair on your head
text:
Although I didn't purchase this kit on Amazon, I'm writing this review so that others who are looking up reviews on this product will have a point of reference. At this point in time there are no reviews on Amazon for this product. I purchased this at Big Lots a few days ago and decided to try it out tonight. It is a 17 piece set that includes 6 rollers, 3 of one size and 3 of another, one really tiny detangler comb, and the rest of the kit are bobby pins. I washed my hair and upon trying to be detangler comb I found it was completely useless so I used my own full sized comb and sectioned a portion of the top of my hair and rolled it in one of the smaller rollers. I didn't feel like it was sitting properly so I tried to unroll it to reposition it, which is something that you can easily do with any other roller I have ever tried, and yet this kit has self adhesive rollers that completely grabbed onto my hair and I could not unroll it. I ended up having to rip hair out of my head in order to get the roller off of my head. I am just going to throw this kir awat because I cannot lose hair just to get some beach waves. I have no idea what kind of hair goody used to test this product out, but it's completely useless. I've been a fan of most of their other products but definitely not of this one.

Just for reference I have medium length, thin, straight, fine brown hair. I wasn't working with any kind of strange textures (coarse, thick, unruly, curly, crimped, etc) that typically would grab on to rollers.

title: Great at washing brushes, ok for drying
text: This product is great as an all in one to wash brushes and dry them. I used to always have soap left in my brush, but the refining section ensures that there is no soap left after a few seconds of scrubbing. Like others have mentioned, it does take a few tries to load the brushes in there correctly and they will fall out a few times. One downfall with this product is the fact that the smaller brushes in the middle never really dry properly. I washed my brushes 3 days ago, had the product with my brushes sitting on a dish drying mat, and most of the brushes inside the small brush holding part are still quite wet. Some larger brushes will not fit in this product. Also DO NOT wash your beauty blender on this. This product rips up my beauty blender so that there are chunks of foam missing. I don’t regret my purchase, in fact I am quite happy that it dries my larger brushes effectively and thoroughly helps to wash my makeup brushes. If you’re thinking about it, buy it. Just maybe don’t use the inside brush holder, dry your tiny brushes elsewhere.

title: Nice but not for sensitive skin
text:
This is the first time that I've used a mask from this line. And I have to admit that the packaging influenced my purchase:)
I really like the feel of the serum and the light fragrance. I, unlike other reviewers, couldn't fit it over my ears. Also the eye holes were way too small. Tip: use the peeI off backing and laid it on my neck and chest.
Left it on for 10 minutes and when I removed it I have small bumps all over my face, but now after 30 minutes, they seem to be going away. Once the serum dries it does feel a little tight on your face but it does not dry out the skin. I finished with a rich moisturizer to "seal the deal." The next morning my face felt very soft but still had a few bumps around the mouth area. My advice would be for those who have dry, sensitive skin, might want to be weary of this mask. Not the mask's fault, mind you, just not a good match for my skin. I would have given it 5 stars but took one off because of the bumps...

title: In less than 24 hours I'm obsessed!
text:
I've had this day cream for less than 24 hours... so I may be jumping the gun but I'm already so amazed I just had to review!

I am in my mid-20's with sensitive dry skin that breaks out in zits and/or hives if you simply just look at it wrong. My previous face cream has either been discontinued or moved to some obscure unfindable website. So I took a leap of faith trying this rose day cream. And oh my goodness, I am so happy I did! My face has never been so soft and clear and... glowy! I feel like I have an entirely new face, and in under 24 hours no less!

This cream is very concentrated. I only need to gently tap the cream with my fingertip once or twice to cover my entire face and neck. It may smell like something your grandmother or great grandmother would have enjoyed... if your grandmother had good taste. It is an absolutely delightful true rose smell (reminds you of the roses your beau would give you) and not at all overpowering. The cream feels a bit greasy at first and you may have to wait a minute to be able to apply make up afterwards but once it settles in, it takes about as long as it does to towel dry your hair, your face feels and looks like dewy perfection. I can't believe nobody told me about this stuff! I guess I'll have to start telling my friends about it myself. I wish I had before and after pictures to show you what a difference it made.

Upon further investigation of the company, Rose Valley Land, I'm now pining after their eye cream and shower gel. I may end up buying every product they have over the next few months as I finish off what I already have.

title: Such Pretty Extensions!
text:
These are really pretty. They look natural. Not shiny. They blend in well with my hair. I found that 1 set was enough, you couldn’t see the clips at all, and my hair isn’t really thick but not extremely thin. They’re easy to clip in and they feel like they’ll stay put, not loose at all.

One thing I didn’t like was that they were a pain to open from the packaging. They had plastic ties and netting and it was a pain getting it all off because I was afraid of damaging the extensions. But I worked slowly and was able to do it. Another thing was they got pretty tangled, but once I saw how long they were it wasn’t a huge surprise. As long as you brush them out good, they are okay.

If you’re looking for a little thicker, fuller, and/or longer hair, I’d recommend these! They are great quality for such an affordable price!

title: Seriously Questioning Whether This is a Genuine Product
text: I have been using Paul Mitchell Tea Tree Lemon Sage Thickening Shampoo and Conditioner for a couple of years.  It has always performed as expected and made my thin, fine hair more manageable and a little fuller feeling.  The last time I ordered it, however, it did not perform or even smell the same as previous purchases.  My hair did not feel clean after shampooing, or more manageable.  In fact, by day two it looked a little greasy.  I subsequently developed dermatitis around my hairline and down my neck to the point where I had to consult a dermatologist.  She suggested it could be my shampoo and told me to stop using this product.  My dermatitis went away and after about a month, I tried using the Paul Mitchell Tea Tree Lemon Sage Shampoo and Conditioner again.  My dermatitis returned almost immediately.  While I suppose I could have developed an allergy to a product I have been using for a couple of years, I question whether this product sold on Amazon is genuine.  I will not order any more cosmetic products from Amazon due to their self-admitted counterfeit problem. Caveat Emptor.

title: Very good, but the pro-health is better
text:
I've been brushing my teeth wih oral-b for over 10 years now, and they are all similar to the vitality model. I've always used the least expensive model since I see no difference from the expensive ones, unless you are looking for "extras", like timer, lcd screen, etc. The vitality is a very good brush. I use the round head instead of the elongated. I rather brush my teeth with oral-b than any other eletric brush on the market. The sonic kind hurts my gum and it tickles a lot as well. Oral-B, and their entry level eletric brushes are the way to go. I can't brush my teeth with regular toothbrush anymore. Brush heads are cheaper on ebay but I found out they don't last as long as those you buy at grocery stores around $20 for 3. I buy the precision clean brush in bulk on ebay for around $1 each. They last a good 2 months. The ones at walmart/amazon and such last 3 months or more. The bad thing about the vitality rechargeable and all the older oral-b I own is that you need to recharge it after 3-4 days of 2-3 daily brushings. I think this is annoying. I didn't want to leave the brush recharging after every use. So I bought the ORAL-B Pro-Health that uses two AA batteries. It is simply AMAZING. The quality is the same as the rechargeables, but it is even better. The batteries lasts forever on the pro-health. I put 2 rechargeable AA (2000 MA) and the thing kept going and going without a noticeable lack of power for a good 2 months. It is the best. The vitality rechargeable brush gets very slow after 3-4 days. Not with the pro-health. Buy the pro-health. It is the way to go if you like this kind of ORAL-B design. Another thing is ORAL-B equipment lasts forever. I only have one eletric brush where the original rechargeable battery died on me. And since they are the entry level brushes, I just buy another one of the same. No need to buy another battery.
Again, go with the ORAL-B PRO-HEALTH eletric toothbrush.

title: Not bad minus the lack of volume :(
text:
I needed a new gray wig and after browsing through the same popular brands on Amazon I decided to take a chance and give this wig a shot...regardless of having only one (one star) review. But I’m glad I did though! The other reviewer mentioned that this unit was too thin but if you look at the pictures I posted, I can assure you it’s not. And the color was spot on; It was the perfect amount of gray and silver and platinum that changed shades depending on the different lighting. The part was decent but still needed some extra plucking and some concealer to blend the lace. (I didn’t have it glued down for the photos so you can still see the lace). As for the styling, personally I like more of a beach or body wave so I need to redo the style but I did straighten it out a bit to relax the curls and it took the heat very well.
I only had two complaints: the roots laid sooo flat and the hairline was way too round. The hairline was pre plucked but way too evenly thinned so it still looked kinda fake and the roundness had me pluckin hairs for what seemed like hours lol. I guess I could YouTube some tricks on how to give this girl some volume because now that’s the only thing missing from her!
For the price, you get more than what you paid for...just be ready to spend some quality time giving her some TLC :)

title: Cell Perfex Review
text:
This was my first time using a cellulite cream so I was a little intimidated. The company recommends using this product immediately after showering and to focus on the targeted area for 60-90 seconds. Now this is a pain when my targeted area is my thighs, specifically the back of my thighs. So it was hard to focus on this area for a minute or longer. There is a slight fragrance to the product but not overwhelming. The texture feels creamy and absorbs into the skin. This product promises to firm, tighten, smooth and inhibit the formation of cellulite, quickly. I did not see immediate results after the first two uses however at 10 days into using this product I can see some results. The area that is receiving treatment feels firmer and smoother. I will continue to use this product in hopes that the tightening begins soon and no new formation of cellulite. The amount needed is a small amount so this container will last a long time. Overall I am a fan. With the results I am able to see already and the promise of more and at this rate I believe this will be a product that keeps its promise. The only reason I did not provide a 5 star review was due to the length of time needed to continuously rub the product into the skin (especially those harder to reach areas). Otherwise I was happy with this product. I received this product in exchange for my honest review
~Kathy

title: Meh but ok
text:
I wish I had known it was so small. I have tiny hands and it fit in my hands perfectly. Was a bit smaller actually. The tattoos that were advertised for Selena’s revival promo were much bigger. But I couldn’t afford them at the time.

I also found out the hard way that if I accidentally moved the part of my body I’m doing the tattoo on just a bit, the tattoo will break apart from each other and become distorted. The G didn’t stay well on my finger. But I didn’t read the part of washing it with soap and water to help it. When I tried the Roman numerals, it finally stayed. I had to wait until about a half an hour after I applied it, keeping still. Then I very gently washed it with hand soap and lukewarm water. Then I dabbed it with a towel and put hairspray on it. It’s so far stayed for up to 8 days in a row. Fading of course, but it’s still going strong. Even with the regular shower. But I pat the tattoo with my scrubbie when I use body wash. So far that’s the only one that’s stayed. The god strengthens me and the date next to the Roman numerals failed to stay.

I’m saving the Arabic and music note because they’re my favorite. Hopefully they’ll finally stay. But unless you’ve got the time, patience and money to be able to do these, I don’t suggest buying them. Unless they’re like $2, I probably won’t be buying them again

title: Great Price, Some Decent Ingredients, but Some Bad Ingredients as Well
text:
I really like products that contain Retinol. They work really well on my oily, acne prone skin. They also work great for combatting fine lines and wrinkles in between Botox injections. Yes, I do like my Botox twice a year.

I like the jar dispenser. It keeps things nice and clean and sanitary and I can control how much product I dispense. This 1.7 oz jar will last a very long time.

I use it at night only for fear of drying my face out, but on rare occasion I have used it in the am and it is a nice cream that glides on smoothly, quickly absorbs and doesn't leave my skin feeling greasy. I am a stickler for that. I won't use a product that makes my face feel greasy, sticky, or tacky.

The amount of Retinol is consistent with that on the market. It also contains Vit B5 and E which are great for repairing damage to the skin and Green Tea.

What I don't like about this product is that while it is 71% organic, it contains Cetyl Alcohol (can dry out the face), Xanthan Gum (a filler) and Phenoxyethanol, which is a very controversial ingredient. It is said to disrupt hormone imbalance amongst other issues. This is very important to me because I am a Breast Cancer Survivor. While I tested negative for hormone receptors and hormone driven cancer, I still proceed with caution. My ob won't even put me on Birth Control because of the hormones. So for me, the ingredients would make me steer away from this product in the future.

title: Easy to fill and easy to use!
text:
My daughter loves using Chapstick, so I decided this would be a fun rainy day activity to do with her! Since these were for my daughter, I wanted to keep them fun with color and "flavor." We made our Chapstick with Kool-Aid! I used Kool-Aid specifically because these tubes are clear and I love seeing the color through them. These containers were fairly easy to fill. It takes a little more coordination than just a lip balm tin, but I like it in these containers better (especially since they are clear). They have held up well so far too. The caps stay on tight and the Chapstick "twists up" perfectly. I haven't noticed any other issues.
Here is the Kool-Aid Chapstick recipe we used:
4 Tbs Coconut Oil
3 Tbs Shea Butter
1 Tbs Beeswax, shaved
A Squirt of kool-aid (we used fruit punch)

1. Mix first three ingredients in a microwave save bowl.
2. After blended, heat for 1 minute.
3. Mix in the kool-aid until desired colour is reached.
4. Pour mixture into your containers and pop it into the fridge for an hour or two.

*I think these would make great gifts! Chapstick/lip balm is very easy to make and can be completely customizable which would be perfect for gift giving!*

title: IF your blowdryer is a match for this diffuser, you are going to want it for lift and volume. Nothing else compares.
text:
Several years ago I had purchased a blowdryer with a similar attachment. I absolutely adored that thing - you could use it on the top of your hair or the sides to give lift so your hair had volume without using any product or doing any backbrushing. The thing was a miracle worker!

Then we entered the era of pin straight hair, and volume and body were out. Thank God, we seem to be passing that era, but hairstyling implements seem to be lagging behind. I was dying to try this diffuser to see if it emulated the gizmo from long ago, and it does. Well made hair styling tool that gives lift and volume to your hair, I love it. Well, kinda.

I'm not sure how 'universal' this is. I tried it on my current blowdryer, and it didn't fit. I had to go digging around until I finally found a Conair dryer upon which it fit, and the funny thing is that it fit like it was MADE for the dryer. The odd thing is that the Conair looks to have the same kind of nozzle the other blowdryer had, so go figger.

IF you have a blowdryer that marries this diffuser well, this is definitely something you're going to want in your hair arsenal. It'd almost be worth getting a new dryer just to use this, it's that good (one star off for the weirdess of the 'universal' claim.)

text:
I know that a lot of people complain about the length of these sticks compared to the type that has a wooden stick involved . Because they are solid incense and the particles aren’t wrapped around a stick , the actual amount of incense to burn is concentrated and more solid incense so it makes up for being more condensed and shorter .
I have been buying this brand for years and years and years, well before we had Internet and way before Amazon prime was even a thing AND ( What’s important ) have always loved the way that these leave my space smelling . title: Helpful hand lotion
text: This product is surprisingly thin and absorbs quickly into skin. My hands are prone to splitting and dry skin areas thanks to poor sloughing off of dead skin cells courtesy of a genetic skin condition.  I also spend a lot of time with my hands in water or washing my hands all day and my knockles always crack, especially in the winter and/or if I have used many liquid hand soaps. This crack repair "lotion" does make a difference quickly if you use at least  2x a day. If I missed an application, I could tell since the fingers started to split again. The formula stung slightly on some or the deeper cuts on my hands, but it wasn't too bad. I put this product on  first and then put on my regular hand lotion. My only dislike is the smell of this product. It doesn't smell gross, but doesn't smell pleasant either. It absorb so quickly that I sometimes have to apply a double dose lately with dry heat running in the house.  The small size tube is easy to stash in a purse, pocket or desk drawer.

title: Decent shaver
text: I bought this to replace my older Norelco 965RX which can no longer hold charge. For the price, it is certainly look and feel a lot cheaper than my 10+ years 965RX. It shave about the same as the 965RX but lacks the battery life indicator, longer charge time, larger AC cord and no travel case. The saving grace for this model is that it has a low battery indicator unlike the newer Norelco 73xx or even the 7130/7140. The head is also all plastic with no adjustment (except for the steel blades and combs) unlike the all metal of the old. The head is definitely flexing more than the old but I am not sure if it really cut any closer than the old "Lift and Cut" blades. This shaver have both slots and foil on the shaver combs to get closer shave and I think it helps on short stubbles. It does have a nice feature that my old Norelco could not do: washable head. The trimmer is similar to the old which I think is much better than the version on the expensive Arcitec which is not sliding out at all to be useful.

title: It does the job well enough
text:
It's okay. Works well for the purpose intended. It's just that it feels a little flimsy...I'm almost expecting the mesh part to fall off or unravel, and then experience painful scratches on my back when I'm caught by surprise. It never happens, but because the mesh sponge doesn't feel secured onto the stick in a way that inspires my confidence, I'm always a little nervous showering with this.

The product page says it comes in green but I received a sky blue version in the mail...I'm not complaining, it's just an observation. For the price this is an excellent buy, and it's definitely worth not stretching yourself out crazily in order to adequately soap up your upper and lower back. I've experienced minor sprains that last for maybe 5 to 10 minutes or so because of this. I'm always happy that I haven't done permanent damage and the pain time frame is minimal...but why risk it? Better to get something like this instead.

DISCLAIMER: I received this product at a discount in exchange for honest and unbiased feedback.

title: Made me Break Out a Little
text: I bought this product after seeing a beauty blogger's review about it. Since I have hyper pigmentation from not using sun screen in my youth, I've been trying to find ways to stop or reverse it. I also bought and tried out other products in conjunction with this one as part of a skin care routine, but I noticed that my skin would break out in very small pimples sometimes. After testing and leaving out products to figure out what was making my skin like this, I discovered that when I stopped using this product, I no longer got those small pimples. My skin is somewhat sensitive, so maybe that's one of the reasons, or some ingredient I'm slightly allergic to? I'm not sure. But I wasn't completely surprised as I did notice my face felt just a tad tingly and extra sensitive after using it (even with just one drop, no more than two, each night). During the time I did use it, which was for a few weeks, I have about half the bottle left, I didn't notice my spots lightening or even staying the same. Actually, they got very slightly darker; even though, I used sun screen. I don't necessarily attribute that occurrence to this product though, but certainly I was disappointed that this did nothing for me even after being patient and using it consistently for a while to see its effects. Maybe it'll work for others as the other reviews seem to suggest.

title: Excellent for price, but somewhat misleading advertisement
text:
I just received this dermatoscope yesterday. For the price, it is excellent. It is a simple to use, contact dermatoscope that gives the user a bright, clear image of skin lesions. I like that it is LED since those seem to last forever, and it even has a lifetime guarantee on the light source. It is comfortable to hold and the carrying case fits well in my white coat.

My reason for not giving 5 stars is that the product description claims that it can be used with or without oil. I suppose this is technically true, as I use alcohol as my immersion fluid of choice. However, if this scope is used without any immersion fluid (as implied by "oil-free epiluminescence microscopy"), it is basically just a 10x magnifying glass. The purpose of dermoscopy is to help visualize structures below the stratum corneum. If the light is not polarized (which it is not in this dermatoscope), then an immersion fluid is necessary to reduce light reflection/refraction/scatter by tiny irregularities at the skin surface in order to see the deeper structures. If you want a true oil-free dermatoscope, you will need one with polarized light (note that these are generally much more expensive and do not necessarily produce a better image). I truthfully did not expect the claim "oil-free" to hold true, but purchased it anyway since I don't mind using immersion fluid and the price cannot be beat. Therefore, I'm not disappointed, but if you are considering purchasing this mostly due to the "oil-free" claim, I'd advise against it.

Basically, if you want a dermatoscope that will give you a great image at a great price, this is your product. If you do not want to use oil or another immersion fluid, make sure you purchase a dermatoscope that is polarized.

Update: October 2013 - After using my dermatoscope for three and a half years in residency and private practice dermatology, the contact plate (where the glass contacts the skin) broke. When I contacted the manufacturer they responded quickly and even offered to send me a free replacement. I continue to be pleased with this dermatoscope and the customer service that stands behind it.

title: Good flosser for the money
text:
DESIGN:

The chrome-plated plastic buttons and cap add a nice touch to the look of the flosser, but it feels a little cheap and will probably wear off quickly. The overall fit and finish is good. It has a nice weight to it—not too heavy or too light. It fits rather comfortably in the hand, even small hands, but the placement of the on/off button creates the sensation that you may unintentionally push it. It doesn’t seem to be a problem, though. The charging port comes with a small rubber insert to protect it from water. It works well, but because it is not attached to the unit, it could easily be lost. It does come with an extra one, though, so that it nice. The portability of this cordless flosser is what I like the most.

PERFORMANCE:

The performance is very good. The power of the water is perfect. One thing I did notice, in comparison to my countertop flosser, is that the third level of power on the cordless flosser is about same as the first level of power on the countertop unit. So, it does not seem quite as powerful as the countertop unit, though the advantage of the portability outweighs the slight loss of power. As for me, I don’t use the highest setting on my countertop unit, anyway, so it is not an issue. A great thing about this flosser is that it is quiet. It doesn’t sound like you are using power tools in your bathroom every time you are flossing. Bottom line, I love this flosser. I think it is a great product for the money. The experience is pleasant, and it does its job well. All in all, a good buy.

title: I was surprised
text:
Read my full review here http://www.fashionbeyondforty.com/2014/09/bella-bear-beauty-products-review.html
I am going to be straight up with you all. When I found out I was receiving these products to review I was sort of nervous. For one thing I have not used an eyelash curler since I was a teen. Second of all, I was really not sure at all about the hair remover. It looked like something that would be used in torture. Now that I have used them, (after my daughter took the initial test run and she turned out okay) I love them!

First of all they are pretty tools in pretty boxes! They are so girly and pretty and pink, and red, and I love that! I thought that being pink that my husband would leave them be as he is always using my tools, but nope, he got a splinter woodworking tonight and came out after getting it out of his skin and asked where the pink tweezers came from. I gave him the run down about it and he said they were a lot better than our other tweezers, which for the record were pretty good too!

Now I will say, the hair remover can be a bit painful but no more painful than trying to tweeze those delicate areas. It does the job a lot faster too. I have fair skin and light facial hair so unless in the sun you can barely see it but in the sun I look a bit furry, even at my lip line. I have used the hair remover on my upper lip and corners of my mouth and while it didn't feel great, it was not too bad and now I have a smooth lip! I also did my hairline and face. It works after you get the hang of it which didn't take long to do.

I was surprised with the lash curler. I remember the curlers from my youth, always sticking on my lashes, pulling them out, but I also do not remember them having the nice slick black stuff on them. I guess we have come a long way in non stick surfaces! I also learned my lashes are naturally longer on my right eye than my left. Interesting, never noticed that before. I can now have lovely curly lashes before applying mascara!

Lastly, the tweezers ... nothing can compare to a great tweezer! I hate when the ends do not come together completely, useless. These tweezers are perfect and really get the job done.

I like having my "girly" tools now, they are pretty to use, and I know it seems shallow, but when I have to pluck, tweeze, and remove hair from my face, I prefer to do it with something pretty rather than utilitarian!

And yes for the record I did get this free but I would never review something I do not personally enjoy using.

title: refreshing, hydrating, basic, budget friendly, add to self care themed gift
text:
Good basic sheet mask that feels cooling and hydrating. Feels nice when my rosacea has made my face feel warm (I have visible veins/redness from rosacea; no other issues). Excellent for use in the bathtub, or for a mini nap, or simply to help relaxation. Might even be perfect post skin treatment sessions that may leave skin feeling irritated.

Stocking stuffer!! I love how budget friendly this pack is. I love making little self-care packages to hand out, and while I will buy and use masks in the $5-35 range, I prefer the $1-3 masks for the token give away (think: facial mask, hand cream, lip balm, etc.. tiny cheap self care packages). I also prefer the cheapies only because I am not sure if the recipient will definitely use it, and the higher end masks tend to be more discerning in what it attends to. The more basic masks tend to be good for nearly everyone, and the resulting feeling is at least a fresh, hydrated face. So these are a repurchase for that purpose, as well as for my own use on days I want to keep it simple.

I love that it has lots of moisture. The serum can be patted in all over face, neck, chest, even elbows & hands after.

For all that it is, I give it 5 stars for this level of facial mask.

title: The best trimmer I've yet used.
text:
First off, I have to say I'm severely disappointed with some of the other reviews of this product, as I have found them to be light years from my experience with this trimmer. Thus far, I have found it to be well designed, well built and very easy to use. Also to my surprise, the vaccum works just great! Yes, there are still hairs that hit the sink, but whadda ya want, perfection? The vaccum collects easily two-thirds to three-quarters of the trimmed hair and that's a good thing.

That said, I've found the most impressive aspect of the trimmer is the extended precision trimming head. While not as wide as the standard trimming head, it extends the cutting edge far enough away from the body of the trimmer to provide the easiest and most accurate edge trimming I've ever experienced. Outlining my beard, moustache and sideburns is suddenly a far easier and more precise task. For any guy who either wants or needs cleaner lines on his facial hair, this is an excellent choice!

I'm still getting used to the length selector and how to get the desired look before having to take off the guide comb and switch trimmer heads to get a more precise cut, but I'm sure that will come with time. It operates in much the same way as my previous Norelco trimmer, but the body design of this trimmer fits much better in the hand, and the guide comb assembly provides a much more comfortable trim as I glide it over my chin. It's also a wonderful thing to have the fast 1 hour recharge time!

The only downside I've found so far is some irritation of the skin while using the extended trimmer head. It's a VERY sharp head and can nick you if you're not careful, but I put that in the still-learning-how-to-use-it category. This is truly a great trimmer, so to all my proudly facially hirsute brothers, I say don't believe the nay-sayers and pick this puppy up!

title: Great Color That Lasts for HOURS!
text:
I usually don't wear more than mascara on a daily basis because I'm not a huge fan of my makeup smudging and fading halfway through the day. NYX's glam shadow sticks have completely changed my opinion about wearing eyeshadow on a daily basis and I'm extremely surprised with my results!

I've always found that powder eye shadow fades and smudges after a few hours without using a pricey primer, and even when I do use an eye primer the shadow near the crease of my lid always fades near the end of the day. These shadow sticks are AMAZING for their staying power! I've never had to touch-up my eye makeup when using them, since the pigment stays exactly where I put it. When I first received the package I swatched each stick on my forearm to test the color and see if I would have any reaction to it. Not only did they not irritate my skin, but the color stayed on my arm all night through an hour's worth of dishes and six hours working in a kitchen! I had five colored lines on my arm until the next morning when I finally decided to use makeup remover on them and take them off completely. In over ten years of wearing makeup I've never had that sort of result from any eye shadow from any brand.

These sticks are extremely easy to work with, even with how long they last! I've found it very easy to blend the pigment from these sticks with my usual brushes, and the colors in this pack compliment each other nicely. The colors themselves are somewhat metallic and very rich, even with only one layer. Each color glides on smoothly and evenly, just as easy as using a crayon!

NYX's glam shadow sticks are my new favorite eye shadow and I've actually been brave enough to wear them to work! I did receive these eye shadows at a discount but price doesn't change how long they last, nor does it effect my review.

I also do not think I received all the necessary parts needed to mount it to the wall.
I received the holder, a plastic bag with (2 screws, and 2 plastic wall inserts), and a bracket to tighten onto a ledge and mount to the holder.
I did not receive any type of bracket to mount it to a wall. With the lip on the top of the holder, you cannot use the 2 screws that came in the plastic bag because the holder is to far from the wall.
Does anyone else have this issue, and I am just not understanding how to mount this?
I am a pretty handy guy, and i believe i possess common sense enough to figure it out, but I can't.

title: But I like it, I was looking for VC oil (not ...

But I like it, I was looking for VC oil (not the serum) for deep hydration and coating at the end. VC serum often is harsh and gone bad fast, in my past experience. This time, I store this oil in my refrigerator, and this small bottle has been lasting longer than I thought. (It's been more than 1 month since I started using it.) I use it for morning step 1 (right after washing my face), and night first step (right after washing my face), and final step mixing up with my night cream (Kiehl's Ultra Moisturizing Cream) to prevent my skin from aging - especially glycation and oxidation. The good about this oil is that I only need 1 drop for 1/2 face as it glides very well. I do not apply so much for some areas such as chin, lateral side of forehead and nose as they might get too oily.

title: The odor is AGONY!!! Updated 03/16/2019
text:
UPDATE AS OF 03/16/2019 All the previous info still applies. Now that I am 16 days out from using this product, I want to add that my feet are STILL peeling. I have been using this type (not this particular) product for a couple of years and the entire process is always completed at least by day 12. Thus, besides the horrible odor, this product is not particularly efficient at exfoliating. So, basically there is NOTHING about it that I can recommend.

No matter how good these are at exfoliating, I cannot recommend them due to the offensive/clingy/cloying/LONGLASTING odor. I have used products like this before and there is always some odor during use...but these, well, days later after soaking my feet in baking soda water and then vinegar water, the incredibly floral+chemical odor still wafts around my head--yes, all the way from my feet! It is sickening, offensive,, and embarrassing when I am around other people. Also, the socks I put on after using this product have been washed in hot water and laundry detergent and they are just going to have to be thrown away because the odor DOES NOT come out. I threw away second set of these bootees because I cannot subject myself to the odor again no matter how badly I want my feet to be soft without rough skin. DO NOT BUY if you are sensitive to odors and/or simply do not like strong odors wafting around you for days on end.

title: Good soap, good value
text:
First, I have to echo the other reviewer's sentiment about the picture being misleading. The text does state that you're buying a single stick, but the picture makes it look like you're buying a box of 'em.

I was initially disappointed with my [[ASIN:B002A5OLHQ Arko Shaving Soap Stick]]. I prefer using a brush and mug, so I unwrapped the stick, cut it into shaving soap coins (which I believe are legal tender in Venezuela and Costa Rica, and certain counties in Arkansas), put them into my mug and stuck it in the microwave for 30 seconds to melt them. What a mistake! That's what I do with [[ASIN:B001A3DNY6 Van Der Hagen Deluxe Shave Soap, 2.5-Ounce Boxes (Pack of 12)]], and it works great--the soap liquifies, adheres to the bottom and sides of the mug, and once it resolidifies, I'm good to go. This stuff softened, yes, but it didn't really liquify. Instead, it kind of foamed up and over the top of the mug. I tried to use my knife to moosh it back into the mug, but it resulted in a very rough, craggy top surface. Figured I'd just use the brush on it anyway--eventually I would wear down that rough surface and leave behind a smooth top that would work perfectly.

I'm glad I did! This soap lathered up like nobody's business when I put the warm, moist brush to it. It made a little thinner lather than I usually get from VDH, but despite it's thinness, it wasn't runny at all--it stayed right where I brushed it, and didn't dry out before my razor got to it. My habit is to do three shaves in different directions, and the Arko lather held out for all three.

I used an older blade, an [[ASIN:B001QY8QXM 100 Astra Superior Premium Platinum Double Edge Safety Razor Blades]], probably about two shaves past when I would usually have replace it, but I was being lazy. I kind of expected to nick myself a couple times. But with the Arko shaving soap, that did not happen. My blade glided across my face as smoothly as I could ask. No nicks at all.

As many have mentioned, the scent is kind of strong, and not entirely pleasant. Some have compared it to citronella, which I can understand. I though it was kind of a cross between the original Old Spice scent from when my dad used their cologne, and some industrial chemical smell. VDH is more or less unscented, so I usually mix a few drops of my favorite cologne ([[ASIN:B00AATHC52 ASPEN by Coty]]) into the liquid when it comes out of the microwave, but I didn't think that would be a good idea with Arko. I didn't think the cologne scent would mix well with the cloying scent the soap came with. But while the scent isn't my cup of tea, after you rinse your face well, it's not very strong at all, especially if you wash your face afterward and use a scented after-shave balm.

The bottom line is, for a single stick, the value isn't great. It's a lot more affordable, and just as usable a product, as some of the much more expensive soaps out there, but at the single stick price, plus shipping, you could buy several pucks of Van Der Hagen locally. When you buy more sticks at a time, the per-stick price comes down, and if you can manage to get free shipping in the deal, it starts looking really attractive. The lather is very nice, the scent, not so much, but all in all, you can't go too far wrong with it. I'm dinging it one star only because of the scent. While I would rather it liquify in the microwave like VDH, that's not how the product was designed to be used, so it wouldn't be fair to dock it for that.

title: They work!
text:
First, this product really *does* whiten teeth--it's not a gimmick as some people think. (It uses a peroxide, which is probably how your dentist would do it, although this is a less concentrated form and takes two weeks.) Of course, it only whitens natural teeth, not dental work, but for most people, they should work.

Now, on to the product itself. Some people have complained about them being difficult to apply, but after the first couple strips I didn't have any problems, and even the first time I applied it I didn't fold a strip or mess up anything major. Keep in mind that some tooth sensitivity is normal; personally, mine was only mild at worst and really only noticeable right after I applied a strip, but I know some people who aren't as lucky. If this is you, using a toothpaste for sensitive teeth may help. Also keep in mind that eating, drinking, and probably talking (unless you have better luck than I) are pretty much out of the question while you're wearing these, but I simply did one application on my drive home from work and another later in the night. My only concern was that the strips, especially the top ones, are a bit short and only cover your front eight or so teeth, but for most people that shouldn't be a problem.

A year after my first use (teeth still looking good), I am currently using a generic brand--Equate, available from Wal-Mart for about $15, about $5 less than these--and have to say that I like Crest Whitestrips better because the strips are more flexible and easier to apply, and they stay in place better. The Equate ones have a bit harder plastic and tend to slide off my teeth if I open my mouth. Hardly worth it to save $5.

It is also worth noting there are two other versions of Whitestrips: Premium and Premium Plus. Both contain a more concentrated peroxide that works in less time (7 and 10 days, respectively [the 10-day version is the same as the 7-day version but includes three extra days of strips for extra whitening], as opposed to the 14 of this Classic version) but are more expensive and--based on their reviews--seem to cause more problems with sensitivity and such. Therefore, unless you're in a hurry, I think you'd be fine with these.

In summary, I am happy with this product and think that most people would be, too. Not bad for the price (certainly cheaper than a professional whitening), and they really do whiten!

title: a competent set but not luxurious
text:
Lately there has been a rush of products that want to use the word "luxury" in their title but are not luxury items by consumer standards. This item falls in that category.

All of the tools included are good tools that are well made. You could even describe them as "above average". They don't have any special features, like "soft touch finish" and the nail clippers don't have the side shields that trap the nail clippings and keep them from flying out. But they are well made and do what they are designed to do.

the case, which is described as a "luxurious" case, is ironically the least impressive item in the set. Its certainly not luxury, unless by luxury you mean "bling-y". And it certainly is overloaded with bling. Like anything that is overly shiny it comes off as "gaudy", although, it will be easy to find in your bag when you are traveling. ha ha. The lack of luxury is not limited to the overly shiny appearance, its also not very nice as far as material selection or build design. A nicer case would suit these tools much better.

That being said, I'm still giving it 4 stars, because at the current price of $10, nobody is thinking that it is luxury anyway. As long as you know what you are getting you can be very happy with the set for $10. Its a good value for quality tools.

text: Overall, this wig is stunning. It looked amazing in my pics for Instagram and complimented my alt look perfectly. The hair is wonderfully soft and is very nice to wear. The lace is very hard to see and the knots are very small, so I did not feel the need to bleach them. In addition, none of the wefting is visible and it looks incredibly natural. However, this wig does have its catches. First of all, it tangles super easily, though that is to be expected for a long synthetic wig. Because of this, I took off a star and would only recommend this wig for pictures, not for everyday wear. In addition, it is NOT very heat resistant. After I took these pictures, I tried to style it some more and ended up ruining the wig, even though my iron was on the lowest setting. When heated, the hair gets scraggily and odd-looking so I would only recommend this wig if you do not intend on styling it with heat. Overall, I would recommend this wig under the right circumstances.

title: Very good soap
text:
I would call this a very good soap. Not quite excellent, due to a couple of peeves, but very close. No 5 Star, but 9 out of 10.

The scent is quite enjoyable and is as labeled, a bay rum and orange. While it is not overpowering; I kind of would have preferred the orange to be a bit toned back to let more of the bay rum through. But I would count that more of a personal preference.

It works more or less as one would expect for cleaning. I found that it formed a light lather with just hand rubbing and would need a washcloth to build a thicker foam. After cleaning, it would rinse off easily with no leftover films or feelings other than just clean.

This soap is also very good for a wet shave. While I am relatively new to wet shaving and only have used a puck of Col Conk to compare to. Once that puck of Col Conk was gone, my bar of this soap was just about done and I tossed the last sliver into my bowl. This soap lathers up beautifully thick with a badger hair brush, and then after application, would stay foamed on the face for the full shave. If anything, I would say this soap is superior to the Col Conk for shaving, and the Conk was a good product and is highly rated.
Heck. If Frannie sold this soap in a round cake suitable for a shave bowl, I would probably stick with it for shaving.

title: Extends lashes nicely, but makes my eyes itch
text:
To create fine, long lashes, this mascara does a pretty nice job. Lashes look longer and natural.

The primer has itsy-bitsy pieces of fiber in it that adhere to the lash and the comb aligns them to lengthen the lashes. The bits of fiber kind of freaked me out when I kept thinking there were individual lashes sticking to the primer comb. The fibers are part of the lengthening process. With that said, I'm still not sure I like the idea of putting bits of fiber so close to my eyes. The packaging says nothing about being ophthalmologist tested, hypoallergenic, etc., so I don't think I'd recommend this product to anyone with sensitive eyes. After wearing this mascara all day, my eyes begin itching, and it requires more than water to remove.

I really liked wearing this mascara as my eyelashes looked longer and feathery and very natural, but I'm concerned about the ingredients.

I received this product for free in exchange for this review.

title: Surprisingly Good For A Costume Wig
text:
So I wear and review a lot of wigs and I have started breaking the review down into sections that I think will be helpful.

Color - The color is very similar to the person pictured wearing it in the examples but not the one that is on the mannequin look. This wig is very light silver / grey. It is not that fake, overly shiny texture, and is the same solid color throughout.

Styling- This has an adjustable headband and is fairly easy to fit and style. As it is a costume wig you have to be careful if you are looking to pull it back for anything as the amount of hair in the wig is really designed more for it to be worn as is (which is normal for this kind of price point)

Comfort - This wig is light and comfortable and there is plenty of airflow. This was a little weird around my ears (because my head is a little smaller) but nothing too difficult. This would be suitable to wear all day although I personally wouldn't.

Overall - I really liked it but for what it was - a costume wig. If you are looking for something for a Halloween type look this is perfect.Great for pictures and videos but not one I would probably wear out (other than possibly to a costume party if it completed the look) just because I have others that look way more realistic.

title: A great hairdryer replacement
text:
After my hairdryer died, I decided to try this product to help keep my beard frizz to a minimum. It's a straight forward product that works well. I like to give it about one minute to get warmed up prior to using it, so I just plug it in and turn it on while I'm pre-combing my beard to get any major tangles out.
I've found that it works best for me if I comb slowly, using the lower heat setting. The only reason that I didn't give this product five stars is because the swivel feature on the power cord seems to need a little assistance from me in swiveling it down and out of the way. I'd prefer that the weight of the power cord was enough for the cord to swivel out of the way properly. But, I wouldn't let this one small negative prevent you from giving this product a try.

title: Super Nice Set
text:
I’m a sucker for a good brush set. It’s even better when it comes with case and a sponge!

So the case is very sturdy and feels well made. You can use it as a brush holder and you can use it store brushes. I am using the shorter half as a brush holder and find that it blends in nicely with my other holders.

Now I’ll do a brief breakdown of the brushes and sponge.

The lip brush is great and it blends in my lipsticks really nice.

There are three brushes for eyes- eyeshadow brush, blending brush and angled liner. I have tried these and they are very soft and work very well.

The powder brush is a bit smaller than I would personally like for powder. It’s not quite dense but in a brush that is used for powder products that’s okay. When I’m buffing in the powder I don’t want the brush to be too dense or it will move the foundation around. I’ve been using this brush for blush because of it’s smaller shape and it’s working great.

Now the final two- the foundation brush and the sponge. I decided to do half of my foundation with the brush on one side, and the other half with the sponge on the other side.

The foundation brush is a typical brush of it’s kind. It did not make the foundation look streaky and applied very smooth.

The sponge is the most interesting piece in this set. It doesn’t feel super soft like a Beauty Blender or a Real Techniques sponge. It feels like a softer stress ball and feels a bit rubbery. I’ve never had a sponge like this. I soaked the sponge in water and drained the excess as I would do with any sponge. It felt heavier but did seem to soften a bit and did expand in size. I dabbed the foundation on my face and pressed the sponge around to press the product in and I got a really smooth finish. The sponge did not seem to absorb much product.

Overall I think it’s a very nice set!

I purchased this product at a discount in exchange for an honest review. I did not get paid to do this review and my opinions are 100% honest.

title: Omg, it works!
text:
I thought I would give this heatless hair curler the ultimate challenge - my daughters thick, 3 ft long hair. My girl plans to grow her hair as long as possible. So, it's important to take special care of it, especially because she only allows it to be cut every few years. As much as possible, I discourage her from using any heated stylers. On special occasions, she wants to have it curled, and I've been looking for something that can handle her volume of hair effectively. I saw this product and was doubtful, but hopeful.

We tried it out a day after she shampooed to allow her hair to fully dry. I watched a YouTube video on how to braid her hair onto the ribbon. It wasn't too difficult to do, but I think I'd have a way harder time trying to do my own hair. Surprisingly, I was able to fit all her hair onto the ribbon and tie it on top her head. She claimed it was fairly comfortable and was able to sleep with it on.

The result in picture 3 was better than expected. The unevenness of the curls are due to my inexperience, but in general, I thought her hair came out very pretty. It lasted for a few hours, but I didn't put any product in, so it could’ve lasted a little longer. Highly recommend!

title: Nice compact portable unit
text:
This is a nifty little unit, small and compact enough (it packs inside itself) to carry with you on the road, in the hospital, etc. It doesn't generate a lot of water pressure but there is plenty to get the job done, and the controls are fairly easy to figure out. For anyone who travels or is short on space, this is the way to go.

The company also stands behind it. I lost the power cord (my fault, not theirs) and they sent me another one free of charge. Do keep an eye on it, as they are impossible to find!

title: Wish it Cut Closer
text:
That's the only "negative" thing I have to say about this shaver. I razor my head to a smooth finish and was hoping this shaver would do the trick. Unfortunately it doesn't get it smooth like a razor but it comes close, shorter than peach fuzz.
It holds a charge for a good long time, two weeks of me using it every other day. Keep in mind, that made using it quick, like two minutes or less to get around my head and face.
The shape makes it comfortable to hold and it conforms to the head nicely, gliding over the surface with ease. I am able to easily get around the tops of my ears without difficulty and it is quite gentle on the skin. Every once in a while I can feel it tug on my neck but that is rare. Going over my head, i don't feel a thing.
I only use it in the shower so as soon as I'm done, I pop open the heads and rinse it out. Super easy.
I've only used a couple of the attachments, namely the ear/nose trimmer and the base trimmer. Both work as they should.
Good waterproof shaver and a really good price. I'm glad I purchased this and hope to get a lot of use from it.

title: 50/50 Chance you get a good bottle
text:
Doesn't matter where you buy them, who you buy from, etc. There is a 50% chance you get a good bottle of polish and a 50% chance you get a bad one. I have 3 bottles from 3 different places, 3 different colors (blue white and black) and the blue one is good, the white one is meh, and the black one is completely useless. The white and black have bent brushes, and the white polish is a bit dried out but not the worst, kind of reminds me of using white-out before they changed the formula and it got all watery and they changed to a sponge applicator from a brush. The black one is awful - it's literally just clumps of dried out polish with some occasional damp spots. The blue one is okay, the polish is alright, but I wouldn't recommend using the brushes that came with these but buying a separate skinny brush for line work or whatever it is you want to do.

I should mention I have been using them to fix an enameled piece of jewelry as opposed to doing nails, as that's what the jeweler recommended (nobody local repairs enamel resin and buying enamel is very expensive and you end up with a ton leftover), so a jeweler I trust recommended nail polish with a good resin sealant and that's what I've been doing. Initially I had used an X-Acto knife to cut painter's tape around the shapes of different colors and metal but switched to this pink-colored liquid latex paint I saw on a youtube video for nail designs and it's much easier.

title: Beautiful horsehair brush
text:
This brush is definitely horsehair. I was comparing it to the Vie Long website, and it looked like a mix brush, but this is 100% horse hair.

The handle is beautiful acrylic. The picture doesn't do it much justice. But the bottom half of the handle is clear with what looks like a white cloud or fog swirls. The upper half is a dark red blood like color. Sitting the brush upside down on a brush stand with the light shining on it really brings out the colors. tl, dr; the brush handle is pretty.

The hair is horse hair, so there's not much else to say about that. It's soft like a badger and the bristles are strong like a boar. So the brush handles like a well broken in omega brush.

I will dock my rating one star for the following:
1.) the brush stinks. Stinks worse than a brand new boat brush. Maybe I've been spoiled by Simpsons and Semogue brushes. But this one stunk something mighty.

title: Power motor, doesn't heat up fast and gets the job done.
text: I purchased this clipper and trimmer combo at Walgreens for $19.99. I was looking for something to just knock my hair down low. It gets the job done with ease due to the fact that I have very course thick hair. You might not be impressed with the build of the clippers especially the trimmer. Basically a plastic cheap material and the trimmer feels even more cheap and runs on a AA battery. I haven't tried the trimmer though I imagine it works fine it just doesn't have a lot of real estate to work with, I'd say it's mainly for touching up a mustache or outlining around your lips. The clippers have a powerful motor. I've been using Conair products for years and this is probably one of my favorites besides the beard trimmer I've owned for years. There isn't a dial on the left side to adjust the clippers for fading but on the right side is an adjustment for setting them to full power. I was able to use a set of combs from another Conair clipper that I have that doesn't work so I didn't have to open the new ones that came with this clipper. The clipper feels very light in my hands and comfortable to use. It didn't heat up too fast and when it did it cooled pretty quickly. Overall if you're looking to save a few dollars on the price tag get it at Walgreens. And if you just want knock a lot of hair with ease then this clipper will get the job done and I imagine for pets too with the added combs.

title: Works well overall - but "bristles" vary in length year to year, become brittle and break off too easily.
text:
It is difficult to find a large barreled curling iron/brush. HELEN OF TROY offers a broad range of assorted sizes, and they work well. This is my third or fourth purchase of this same model in about 10 years. Even though I purchase the same model, I find that the hard plastic "bristles" vary in height from year to year. The short "bristles" sometimes place the hot barrel too close to my skin, especially when the "bristles" break off (which is fairly easy and common, possibly due to overexposure to heat). The models that arrived with longer "bristles" are more suited for longer hair, however they sometimes "tangle" in the hair -- and THEY, too break off all too easily. (I have recommended to the manufacturer that they add something slightly more flexible to the "bristles" to keep them from getting brittle and breaking, or perhaps design the barrels so that new sets of bristles can be installed, however -- why would they take my suggestion when consumers can simply replace the entire unit with a new one?) The heating elements in these units have never failed; only the "bristles" are problematic.

Overall, I like this model, but I find that I need to replace it after about 12-18 months of use due to loss of "bristles".

It does not leave your hair feeling oily, it genuinely does clean the hair and absorb oil without leaving any powder on your scalp. As an added bonus it seems to give hair wonderful body, volume and control.  I have a tendency to put it on my hair right after I wash it in order to help me style my hair better.  I will keep this product on hand at all times.  It also smells great and leaves hair smelling wonderfully.  I have fine, thin hair in a chin length platinum blonde bob and this makes my hair look thick and full, and also seems to hold it's shape a couple of days using this product. I definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a product that will do these things. You will not be disappointed. I have tried so many other dry shampoos and this one is literally the best. title: Great shaver, reasonably priced
text:
I bought my first electric shaver about 55 years ago and my first Norelco only a few years later. Since then I've tried them all, and this is by far the best.

I joke that it is a magic shaver, because you don't hear anything and don't feel anything.

That's a slight exaggeration. You do hear a very slight hum, but it it is so quiet that I really thought something was wrong the first time I used it, but I quickly noticed that whereever it touched my face, the whiskers disappeared.

It does cause some minor skin irritation, but it is instantly cured by a few drops of after shave balm.

Very easy to clean, and a charge lasts about two weeks.

Very light, very comfortable in your hand. Can't find a single negative.

title: No nicks, easy to use, easy to charge, quiet...
text:
This razor is nice to look at, easy to hold, feels good, and isn't too loud. It does a good job on some hair and not on others. Summary: works on hair that isn't too long and isn't too short, incredibly well.

I first tried this when I was about 2 days out from having shaved my legs. The razor did not remove most of the stubble - I think it's just to short. However, I have always struggled to shave the back of my thighs in the shower, and thus the hair there is longer and finer. This razor did a good job on that hair, though I had to go over it a few times from different directions - it definately cuts best when going against the grain, and the hair on the back of my thighs and underarms seems to grow at all different angles, so I had to shave from all different angles.

While I don't believe this can become my only razor, I am keeping it because a) it does well on longer, finer hair and is easier to use in areas I can't see well, like the back of my thighs. b) It does a good enough job that I'm keeping it for those times when I got lazy, didn't shave, not suddenly find myself going somewhere and needing a quick dry leg shave. If it just got those shorter hairs better, so I could use it every 2-3 days, I would love it.

title: Re: Cedar, I Think NOT
text:
One thing I cannot stand is the smell of sandlewood. Not only do I hate the smell, but it reacts with my sinuses and causes an allergic reaction.

This "cedar" that I have purchased -- which the bag says is "100% pure" is NOT 100% pure cedar. It REEKS of sandlewood. Trash. And now my sinuses are messed up. Thanks a lot.

title: Easily to apply and you get 50 of them!
text:
Just like my title says, they are easy to apply. The instructions tell you to wet the actual strip and then put it on your face, but I recommend keeping the strip dry and just lightly wet the area around your nose. I tried both ways and for some reason, wetting my face just worked much nicer. You also get 50 of them so thats a huge plus considering the price.

Now the big question.....do they work?
Well. When I used it the two times, it dude pull out some small blackheads and looked like it did a decent job. I've always been a little skeptical of these kinds of products and I don't feel like it does that much in general. I kind of use these for placebo and catharsis. It's comforting to see these strips pull ANYTHING out of your skin, so it makes me feel better about it. But it is completely acne-clearing and black head cleansing? Not really.

These are inexpensive, you get a bunch of them, and they are nice to use. I recommend it but if you have somewhat large pores and oily skin like me, don't expect much passed "Oh.....well that's nice." I wish it did more. But these are okay.
